title: Canva Designer
description:
JOB TITLE: Canva Designer



JOB DESCRIPTION:

- We are a US-based marketing consulting agency that runs done-for-you
 and Instagram ad campaigns for home-service businesses (painters, foundation repair, landscapers, truck repair, remodelers, and more). We are growing fast and need a skilled Canva Designer to help us create scroll-stopping static ad graphics for our clients.

- This is a design-only role. You are not managing campaigns, writing copy, or talking to clients. Your job is to take a creative brief from our team and turn it into polished, high-converting
 and Instagram ad graphics.



What you will be doing:

- Designing static image ads in Canva based on briefs from our marketing team. Each client gets 2-4 new static ads per month, and we have 9+ clients now and growing fast.

- Creating before-and-after graphics, offer graphics, price-point graphics, and lifestyle ads for home-service businesses.

- Designing carousel ads (multi-image) for
 and Instagram.

- Making quick edits and revisions based on feedback.

- Organizing files inside Google Drive so every client's assets are clean and easy to find.

- Following client brand guidelines (logo, colors, fonts) exactly.

- Keeping up with current
 and Instagram ad design trends so our creative performs well.



What we need from you:

- Strong Canva skills. You should be able to design professional ad graphics quickly without constant hand-holding. Send us examples of your work.

- Understanding of what makes a scroll-stopping ad. You know how to grab attention in the
 feed with bold text, strong contrast, and clear offers.

- Good eye for design. Clean layouts, readable text hierarchy, balanced spacing, smart use of color.

- Attention to detail. Client names, prices, phone numbers, logos, and offers must be exactly right every single time.

- Fast turnaround. When we send you a brief, we need the first draft back within 24-48 hours.

- Reliable internet and consistent work hours. You do not need to be on video calls often but you need to be responsive in Slack during our working hours.

- Solid written English. You will be communicating with our team in Slack about briefs and revisions. Your English does not need to be perfect but you need to understand instructions clearly and ask good questions when something is unclear.



Nice to have (not required):

- Experience designing
 or Instagram ads specifically.

- Understanding of ad copy principles (hook, offer, call to action).

- Experience using Canva's Brand Kit feature to manage multiple client brands.

- Basic photo editing skills (background removal, color correction).



Details:

Part-time to full-time depending on workload and your availability. Start at 20 hours per week with room to grow.

Must be available for some overlap with US business hours (Central Time, GMT-6). At least 4 hours of overlap per day.



Long-term position. We are scaling quickly and there is room to grow with us.

Start date: immediately.



To apply:

Send us the following:

5-10 examples of Canva ad graphics or marketing designs you have created. Send a portfolio link or a Google Drive folder.

Take this sample brief and design one
 ad graphic. Send us the result as a JPG or PNG.

Sample brief: Design a
 ad for a Dallas foundation repair company. The offer is "$275 per pier, lifetime warranty, free same-day quotes." Include the company name "Onyx Foundations." Target DFW homeowners.



Your Canva skill level (beginner, intermediate, advanced).

How many hours per week you can commit.

How soon you can start.

title: GHL CRM + Automation Specialist (Real Estate Experience)
description:
We’re a land acquisitions company looking for someone experienced with GoHighLevel (white label) who understands real estate workflows and can build out a clean, simple CRM system.



Our goal is to take our previous CRM (Resimpli) and rebuild it inside GHL using pipelines, workflows, and automations.



We already have a clear vision for how we want the system to function. We need someone who can take that vision and build it out properly.



What we need:



Build and organize pipelines (Comping + Acquisitions)

Set up workflows and automations based on tags and stages

Create a simple, stage-based system (not task-heavy)

Ensure clean lead flow from leads in, to offers out, to closings

Prevent duplicate opportunities and backwards movement

Set up KPI tracking through pipeline movement



System structure:



Texter (AI-driven) qualifies and tags leads

Comper handles comping and valuation

Texter sends offers

Acquisitions calls and closes



Automation & Integration:



Experience with Zapier and/or n8n preferred

Ability to integrate external systems into GHL

Long-term goal is to automate most of the workflow end-to-end

Experience with AI or automated messaging systems is a plus



Contracts:



Set up contract automation using Zapier + DocuSign or PandaDoc

Ensure signatures include IP tracking and certification

Sync contract status back into GHL



Important:

If you DO NOT have experience building real estate CRM systems or similar workflow automations, please DO NOT apply.



Compensation & Scope:

Please include your fixed price for this project and briefly outline what deliverables you would provide.



Deliverables should include:



Fully built pipelines inside GHL

Workflows and automations based on tags and stages

Clean lead flow across Texter, Comper, and Acquisitions

Basic documentation or SOP explaining how the system works



This will start as a paid test project (gig). If completed successfully, there is an opportunity for ongoing work and further system expansion.



To apply, include:



Examples of similar systems you’ve built

Platforms you’ve worked with (GHL, Podio, etc.)

Any experience with automation tools (Zapier, n8n, APIs, etc.)

A brief explanation of how you would set up a workflow in GoHighLevel for this scenario:



A lead replies “Yes” or “Interested” to a text message.



What happens next in the system from:



Tagging the lead

Moving them into a pipeline

Assigning it to a user

Getting it to the Ready for Offer stage



Keep your answer short and focused on logic.



We’re looking for someone who understands systems and can build this cleanly, simply, and in a scalable way.

title: Finance Operations Specialist (Xero, Ecommerce)
description:
*** Looking for someone who prefers a long-term, stable role with clean systems — not juggling multiple clients. ***



We are a fast-growing 7-figure ecommerce brand (Amazon + Shopify), operating with a lean, high-performance team.



We are looking for a highly reliable and detail-oriented Finance Operations Specialist to own our day-to-day finance operations and maintain a clean, structured, and audit-ready financial system.



This role includes ownership of our Accounts Payable workflow (invoice ? payment), ensuring all supplier invoices and payments are properly recorded, matched, and documented.



Expected workload: ~15–20 hours per week (ongoing, long-term).



—



Role Overview



You will be responsible for maintaining accurate, clean, and reliable financial records across Amazon and Shopify, ensuring the business always has clear visibility into profitability, cash flow, and inventory.



—



Key Responsibilities



• Record all supplier invoices as Bills in Xero (with invoice attachments)

• Review supplier invoices for accuracy (price, quantity, totals) before payment

• Ensure all transactions are correctly categorized and complete

• Maintain a clean and consistent chart of accounts (no duplicates)

• Reconcile Amazon, Shopify, and all payment accounts accurately

• Maintain accurate inventory and COGS (accrual-based, aligned with supplier invoices)

• Manage Accounts Payable workflow:

   * Ensure all invoices are recorded properly

   * Prepare payment batches in Airwallex (for approval)

   * Ensure all payments are linked to bills

• Reconcile bank accounts and ensure no unmatched transactions

• Maintain audit-ready records at all times



—



Monthly Responsibilities



• Complete monthly close by the 5th

• Ensure all data is accurate, consistent, and fully reconciled

• Deliver a clean P&L (no manual fixes required)

• Ensure financial data is ready for reporting and decision-making



—



Systems



• Xero (accounting)

• Link My Books / A2X (Amazon integration)

• Airwallex (payments)



—



Requirements



• Strong experience with ecommerce accounting (Amazon + Shopify)

• Proficiency in Xero (required)

• Experience with A2X / Link My Books or similar tools

• Understanding of payment processors (Amazon payouts, Shopify, Stripe, PayPal)

• Strong understanding of accrual accounting (not cash-based shortcuts)

• Experience with inventory and COGS flows

• Ability to reconcile complex, high-volume transactions

• Strong attention to detail and accuracy

• Systems thinker (avoids manual, repetitive work where possible)

• Reliable and consistent (non-negotiable)

• Clear communication and ability to explain numbers simply



—



What We’re Looking For



• Extremely reliable and detail-oriented

• Keeps systems clean, consistent, and easy to understand

• Thinks in systems, not one-off fixes

• Takes ownership and solves problems independently

• Maintains audit-ready records at all times



—



Important



We do not want:



• Manual “one-by-one” work where it can be avoided

• Duplicate or inconsistent accounts

• Missing or unlinked invoices

• Transactions that require explanation or re-checking

• Heavy reliance on support teams to solve basic issues



—



What Success Looks Like



• Xero is always clean and up to date

• All transactions are categorized, matched, and documented

• Amazon and Shopify are fully reconciled with no discrepancies

• P&L is accurate and can be trusted at any time

• Payments are clearly tracked and linked to invoices

• Audit requests can be fulfilled quickly without manual digging



—



To Apply



Only applications with clear answers to the questions below will be reviewed.



1. How would you structure revenue accounts for Amazon and Shopify in Xero?

2. How do you ensure all supplier payments are properly tracked and linked to invoices?

3. If you find duplicate or inconsistent accounts in Xero, how would you fix it?

4. What would you check first when reviewing a new ecommerce company’s books?



We are looking to hire quickly and long-term.

title: Affiliate Manager (Lead Generation – Financial Verticals)
description:
About the Role:

We’re hiring a hands-on Affiliate Manager to scale lead generation campaigns across financial verticals (insurance, home improvement, loans).



This is a performance-focused role - you’ll be responsible for growing affiliate partnerships, managing campaigns, and ensuring high-quality, scalable lead flow with an EPC-first mindset.



Responsibilities:

Recruit, onboard, and activate new affiliates (media buyers, networks, publishers)

Manage and scale relationships with existing partners

Identify high-potential affiliates based on volume and lead quality

Launch and manage lead-gen campaigns across native, display, social, and other traffic sources

Optimize performance through continuous testing and iteration

Manage caps and traffic allocation across partners

Match the right offers to the right traffic sources

Monitor traffic quality and prevent fraud or low-intent leads

Handle creative and content pre-approvals



Requirements (Must-Have):

Experience with RedTrack and Affise

Strong technical skills (postback setup, tracking, troubleshooting)

Experience in affiliate / performance marketing

Proven experience in lead generation campaigns

Strong understanding of RevShare / CPL models and affiliate economics (EPC-first mindset)

Existing network of affiliates in lead-gen

High attention to detail

High availability (responsive, able to manage time-sensitive campaigns and partner communication)





Important – Required to Apply:

To be considered for this role, you must answer the following questions. Applications without answers will not be reviewed.



1. Describe your experience in affiliate / performance marketing. What verticals have you worked in?

2. What lead generation campaigns have you managed? (insurance, loans, home improvement, etc.)

3. What was your exact role - affiliate manager, media buyer, or both? What were your day-to-day responsibilities?

4. Explain your experience with RedTrack and Affise. What did you use each for?

5. Have you set up postbacks before? Briefly explain how you do it.

6. How do you find and recruit new affiliates? Be specific.

7. Do you have an existing affiliate connections? If yes, describe it (types of affiliates, GEOs, verticals).

8. What metrics do you focus on when optimizing campaigns? How do you balance lead quality vs volume?

9. If an affiliate is sending high volume but low-quality leads, what actions would you take?

title: ## Job Opportunity: Executive Assistant (Life & Business Operations)
description:
We are looking for a high-level executive Assistant (EA) who doesn't just manage a calendar, but masters it. This role is designed for a proactive "right hand" capable of navigating the intersection of personal logistics and multi-business operations.



As my EA, your primary mission is to **buy back my time.** You will be the gatekeeper, the air traffic controller, and the connective tissue between my various ventures and my personal life.



 Key Responsibilities

1.
 & Communication Management

 Inbox Zero: Filter, prioritize, and draft responses for multiple
 accounts.

 Communication Hub: Act as the first point of contact for internal teams and external partners.

 Tone Matching: Learn my voice to handle routine correspondence with speed and authenticity.

2. Calendar Defense & Appointment Setting

 The Gatekeeper:Protect my deep-work blocks and ensure my schedule aligns with current priorities.

 Logistics Pro: Coordinate complex scheduling across multiple time zones, including personal appointments and family logistics.

Prep & Briefing: Ensure I have all necessary documents and context before stepping into any meeting.

3. Business Operations & Team Integration

 Liaison: Work directly with my leadership teams across different businesses to track project statuses and deliverables.

 Task Management: Own the "to-do" list. Assign tasks to relevant tea
mbers and follow up to ensure completion.

Meeting Mastery:  Attend key meetings, record minutes, and transform discussions into actionable task lists.



4. Personal Life Management

  Manage travel arrangements, dining reservations, and personal errands.

 Handle sensitive information with absolute discretion.



Who You Are

 Hyper-Organized: You find peace in a clean spreadsheet and an optimized calendar.



The "Buffer":You are firm but professional. You know how to say "no" on my behalf so I don’t have to.

 Context Switcher: You can jump from a real estate discussion to a tech startup
 without losing a beat.



Tech Savvy: Proficient in AI, 
, Google Workspace, and AI productivity tools.



Low Friction:You solve problems before they reach my desk. You don’t just bring problems; you bring three potential solutions.



 Qualifications & Requirements

| Requirement | Description |



| **Experience** | 3+ years as an EA or Operations Manager for a high-net-worth individual or founder. |

| **Availability** | Responsive during core business hours with occasional "on-call" flexibility for urgent matters. |

| **Confidentiality** | Must sign a comprehensive NDA; discretion is non-negotiable. |

| **Communication** | Exceptional written and verbal English skills. |

> **"Your job is to manage the noise so I can focus on the signal."**



 How to Apply

If you are the type of person who stays three steps ahead and takes pride in making a chaotic life run like a Swiss watch, please send your resume and a video. In your video, tell me three things:

1- why do you want this job and why do you think you’ll do great at this job?

2- what is a time you didn’t manage to do what was asked of you and how you handled it.

3- tell me a time you were not able to make it to work and how you handled it.

title: Amazon SEO Specialist
description:
I run a fast growing Amazon PPC agency that manages 20+ 7-8 figure Amazon brands, and I'm looking for a part-time SEO specialist to help my clients rank and convert better on Amazon.

                                                                                

  What you'll be doing:                                     

  - Keyword research using tools like Helium 10, DataDive, or SmartScout

  - Writing and optimising titles, bullets, descriptions, and backend keywords  

  - Indexing and rank checks, then fixing what's not indexing                 

  - Competitor listing teardowns to find gaps we can exploit                    

  - Working with Seach Query Performance report and search term data to prioritise which keywords actually matter                                                                        

  - Coordinating with our catalog person to push listing changes live          

                                                                                

  About you:                                                                    

  - Amazon SEO experience, not Google SEO. They're different games.

  - You understand the difference between ranking for a keyword and actually converting on it                                                          

  - Comfortable reading SQP reports, STR exports, and PPC data to inform listing changes                                                                      

  - You can write listings that read like a human wrote them, optimized for conversion rate too, not just a keyword-stuffed robot                                     

  - Self-directed and organised                                                 

                                                            

  The role:                                                                     

  - Part-time, ongoing, growing as we grow                                     

  - Work across a handful of client accounts                                    

  - Reporting directly to me

  3. Your hourly rate and availability

                                                                                

  Thanks,

  Jake

title: AI Systems & Automation Lead - Claude - (Ecommerce | High-Growth Brand)
description:
AI Systems & Automation Lead (Ecommerce | High-Growth Brand) 



Location: Remote

Type: Full-Time / Contract (flexible)



About Us



We are a fast-growing, founder-led eCommerce brand on a mission to help people live healthier through clean eating—without added sugars. 



We operate lean and want to add someone who can help build smarter systems using AI and automation so our small team can perform like a much larger one.



The Role



We’re hiring an AI Systems & Automation Lead to identify inefficiencies across the business and build solutions using tools like Claude, GPT, Zapier, APIs, and internal workflows.



Your job is simple (but not easy):

???? Turn messy processes into scalable systems

???? Use AI to eliminate manual work and improve decision-making



This is not just a “prompting” role—this is about thinking like an operator and building leverage across the business.



What You’ll Do



I want you to come in and give us perspective on how we can help improve utilizing AI. There's things we use it for already using it for but maybe not in the correct system. I want someone who has experience with helping companies. It will not be a fit if you don't know what is proven to work. 



What Success Looks Like:



Our team saves 10–20+ hours per week through automation

Faster decision-making across marketing & ops

Clear, documented systems that reduce chaos

AI becomes embedded into daily workflows—not a side tool



Who You Are:



Obsessed with efficiency, leverage, and speed

Strong understanding of eCommerce operations (Shopify)

Experience with AI tools like Claude or GPT

Bonus: experience with APIs, automation tools, or light coding

Nice-to-Have Experience

Shopify + DTC brands

Meta ads workflows / creative testing systems

Building internal tools for small teams



Why This Role Matters



We’re not trying to build a big team—we’re trying to build a smart team.



This role will directly impact how fast we grow by:



Increasing output without increasing headcount

Improving margins through smarter systems

Giving the founder leverage across every part of the business



How to Apply



Send us:



A short note on how you think about systems + AI leverage

1–2 examples of systems or automations you’ve built



(Optional) A quick Loom breaking down how you’d improve a messy workflow

Compensation



Flexible based on experience (base + performance upside possible)



Optional Add-On (this will dramatically improve applicants)



Test Project (Optional but Preferred)



We care more about how you think than perfect answers.

title: Afterhours Dispatch/ Customer Service Coordinators
description:
After-Hours Dispatcher & Customer Service Coordinator – Remote

Pay: $3–$6 per hour

Full-time | Long-term

US Hours (Mountain Standard Time)

Available Shifts:

• 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM (MST)

• 12:00 AM – 8:00 AM (MST)



Applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed.



Inbound Calls | Emergency Dispatch | Appointment Scheduling



We are hiring a confident, professional English-speaking Customer Service & Dispatch Coordinator to manage inbound calls and dispatch technicians during our after-hours service window.

This role is critical to our operations. You will be the first point of contact for customers experiencing urgent plumbing, HVAC, or electrical issues after hours, and you will coordinate technicians to respond quickly and professionally.

You will represent our company on every call. Strong, natural English communication is not optional. You must be able to speak clearly, understand nuance, and communicate with confidence and professionalism.

This role requires someone who can remain calm under pressure, prioritize urgent calls, and coordinate technicians efficiently.



Key Responsibilities



Customer Service

Answer inbound customer calls promptly and professionally

Respond to follow-up questions via phone and text

Provide reassurance and clear communication to customers experiencing urgent home service issues

Follow qualification scripts accurately

Book appointments directly into the calendar when calls are non-emergency

Dispatch Responsibilities

Dispatch on-call technicians for emergency service calls

Communicate job details clearly to technicians

Coordinate technician availability and routing

Monitor service call progress and update customers when necessary

Escalate urgent issues to management when needed

CRM & Documentation

Update detailed notes in our CRM

Record customer concerns accurately

Document call outcomes and dispatch status

Maintain organized records of after-hours service calls



Performance Expectations



Maintain fast response times on inbound calls

Follow company dispatch protocols

Maintain professional, calm communication during emergencies

Meet daily call handling and scheduling targets

Requirements (Please Read Carefully)

C2-level or near-native English fluency (required)

Clear, professional speaking voice

Strong listening skills and conversational awareness

Prior customer service, appointment setting, call center, or dispatch experience

Comfortable speaking with homeowners and business owners

Ability to remain calm and organized during urgent service calls

Reliable high-speed internet

Quiet, professional home office setup

Coachable, dependable, and detail-oriented

This role requires someone who can sound natural and confident on the phone. If English is difficult or heavily accented to the point of impacting clarity, this role will not be a fit.



Compensation & Position Details



$3–$6 per hour, based on experience and performance

Full-time position with consistent hours

After-hours shift coverage (evenings or overnight)

Long-term opportunity for the right candidate

Performance-based bonuses available after ramp-up period

Ideal Candidate

The ideal candidate is someone who:

Speaks clear, professional English

Can stay calm while handling urgent service calls

Is organized and capable of managing multiple technicians

Has strong customer service instincts

Takes pride in providing excellent customer experiences

How to Apply

To be considered, please submit:

A short voice recording introducing yourself (required)

A brief explanation of your customer service, call center, or dispatch experience

Your availability (including which shift you prefer)

Your internet speed

Applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed.

This is a stable, long-term position with clear expectations and measurable goals. We are looking for someone professional, confident, and serious about performing at a high level.

If that describes you, apply.

 with:

title: Ecommerce Manager bol.com / Growth Partner at Membeli Group
description:

Membeli Group is a fast-growing European ecommerce company managing 12 private label brands across lifestyle and wellness product categories. We sell on mainly on 
. Our team is young, ambitious, and driven to build great brands, and we are looking for a long-term tea
mber who wants to grow with us.



About the Role

We are looking for a reliable, proactive, and detail-oriented Ecommerce Manager to help run and grow our business. This is a full-time remote position, and we are looking for someone who wants to stay with us for the long haul — ideally 2+ years. As our business grows, so will your role and responsibilities.



The role has two main parts:



1. Daily Operations (~20 hours/week)

Monitor and analyze sales performance on 
 and report findings with actionable recommendations.

Manage inventory levels and coordinate purchase orders with suppliers in China.

Manage and optimize PPC advertising campaigns on 
.

Analyze customer questions and reviews to identify product issues and feed insights back into product improvement.

Coordinate with sourcing agents and manage the ordering process.



2. Growth & Innovation (~20 hours/week)

Launch new products on 
: from sourcing and packaging development to listing optimization.

Build AI systems to automate parts of the business

Optimize product content and conversion (photos, copy, ads).

Research new markets such as Germany and develop localized strategies.

Source new products, develop unique packaging, and add differentiating features.

Improve brand experience through custom packaging,
 flows, and review optimization.

Help prepare and implement a B2B strategy for partnerships with physical retailers.

Support the development of an in-house agency for our own brands and future external clients.



What We Offer

$1,000 per month (full-time, 40 hours/week).

Performance bonus based on revenue targets — the better we do, the more you earn.

100% remote

A long-term position with real growth potential: as the company grows, your role and compensation grow with it.

Lots of ownership and freedom — we trust you to get things done.

Direct collaboration with the founder.



Who We Are Looking For

You have experience with ecommerce, preferably 
 or Amazon

You are highly reliable — you do what you say you will do, and you communicate early if something is not going as planned.

You are proactive and self-directed: you do not wait to be told what to do next.

You are comfortable working independently without daily supervision.

You have strong analytical skills and enjoy working with data.

You communicate clearly and consistently, you keep your collegeaus informed without being asked.

You are ambitious and looking for a long-term opportunity, not just a short-term job.

You have a stable internet connection and a reliable work setup at home.



Important: What We Value Most

We are a small, remote team and we rely heavily on trust. The most important qualities for this role are reliability and communication. We do not micromanage, but we do expect you to be reachable during agreed working hours, to meet your commitments, and to flag problems early rather than going quiet. If you are someone who thrives with independence and takes pride in their work, you will love it here.



How to Apply

only
 applications get checked. Do not reply via onlinejobs. Please send your application to 
 your application, include the following:



1. A short introduction about yourself and specifcally state your ecommerce experience. If you have worked with 
 before. State this.

3. An example of a time you worked fully remotely and how you stayed organized and on track.

We look forward to hearing from you!

title: GTM Engineer for B2B Platform

This role sits at the intersection of sales, marketing, operations, data, and AI. You’ll be responsible for building the infrastructure that helps us generate demand, improve conversion, move faster, and make better decisions. From automating workflows and connecting tools to building with AI and surfacing insights, you’ll play a key role in driving growth.



What you’ll do

Build and maintain the technical infrastructure behind our go-to-market function.

Use AI tools and workflows to improve how we generate leads, qualify opportunities, personalise outreach.



Build automations across sales, marketing, and operations to reduce manual work and increase speed

Own CRM systems, workflows, integrations, and data hygiene

Experience in a GTM Engineering, RevOps, Sales Ops, Growth Ops, or similar role

Strong interest in building with AI and using it practically to improve business performance

Comfortable working with integrations, APIs, data, and automation tools

Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to move quickly and independently

Able to work across technical and non-technical teams

Commercially minded and focused on building systems that drive revenue





Experience building AI agents, internal tools, or AI-assisted workflows

SQL, JavaScript, or Python experience

Experience in high-growth startups or fast-moving environments

Familiarity with enrichment, lead scoring, attribution, and outbound systems

Strong understanding of how AI can improve GTM execution without adding unnecessary complexity

Please include GTM2026 in the subject of this application so I know that you read the job description





Opportunity to build and shape the GTM engine from the ground up

Chance to work at the front edge of AI-powered go-to-market

High ownership and direct impact on company growth

Fast-moving environment with room to experiment, test, and improve

title: Advanced Credit Repair Dispute Specialist
description:
We are not looking for basic credit repair specialists.



We are looking for someone who understands advanced dispute strategy, consumer law, and how to produce real, measurable results — not just send templates.



This role is for someone who knows how to get deletions, think strategically across dispute rounds, and move fast while maintaining accuracy.



If you rely on generic templates or surface-level disputes, this is not the role for you.



What You’ll Be Doing



* Managing client credit profiles and executing dispute rounds

* Disputing directly through credit bureau portals (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)

* Filing and managing CFPB complaints and escalations

* Using advanced dispute strategies (FCRA, FDCPA, Metro 2, factual disputes, etc.)

* Handling all account types including:

    * Collections

    * Charge-offs

    * Late payments

    * Repossessions

    * Inquiries

    * Bankruptcies

    * Closed and open accounts

* Running credit sweep strategies when appropriate

* Updating client progress and maintaining clean, accurate records

* Working within our internal systems (GHL + DisputeFox workflows)



What We’re Looking For



* Strong understanding of FCRA, FDCPA, and consumer law

* Proven experience disputing through bureau portals and CFPB

* Proven success with both credit sweep strategies and advanced dispute strategies (this is required)

* Ability to handle all account types and structure disputes accordingly

* Familiarity with DisputeFox

* Ability to produce fast, clean, and permanent deletions

* Strong attention to detail and ability to manage volume

* Self-starter who can operate without constant oversight



Compensation



* Base pay is based on experience

* Performance-based increases tied to results and volume handled

* Opportunity to grow into full time and higher pay as you demonstrate consistent performance



We are building a long-term team. The more value you bring and the more volume you can handle efficiently, the more you will earn.



To Apply (Required)



Please answer the following:



1. What credit sweep strategies have you successfully used?

2. What advanced dispute strategies do you use beyond basic disputes?

3.. What tools/software have you used (DisputeFox, CRC, etc.)?

4.     On average, how long have you seen it take to get results using credit sweep strategies vs advanced disputing? (We understand this varies by profile)



Additional Notes



Experience with DisputeFox, Metro 2 compliance, CFPB escalation, and litigation-based dispute strategies is a strong plus.



This role is for someone who takes pride in results, moves with urgency, and wants to grow with a serious operation.

title: Senior Checkout Champ Funnel Builder & Designer (Long-Term Specialist)
description:
We are a growing brand looking for an experienced, creative Checkout Champ specialist to join our team on a long-term, ongoing basis.



You will be given complete landing page copy documents that include all the headlines, subheadlines, body copy, offers, testimonials, guarantees, and sales psychology needed for each funnel. You will also receive inspiration pieces for every project (reference funnels, mood boards, competitor examples, brand guidelines, color palettes, and desired aesthetic direction).



Your job is to transform that copy and inspiration into high-converting, visually compelling Checkout Champ funnels. You must independently think through and execute the full visual design: searching online for the right stock images, lifestyle photography, and product shots; using AI image generation tools to create custom visuals when needed; and leveraging design tools to produce professional graphics, layouts, buttons, icons, and branded elements.



This is not a one-time project. We move fast, launch new offers regularly, and need someone who will be a consistent, reliable part of the team as funnel building, testing, and optimization is ongoing.



Key Responsibilities



- Build brand-new sales funnels and replicate or improve upon existing high-performing pages in Checkout Champ with pixel-perfect accuracy.

- Take the provided copy document and independently design the complete user experience — including pre-sell pages, video sales letters, checkout pages, upsells, downsells, order bumps, and thank-you pages.

- Independently handle all visual and design elements:

- Search online (stock libraries, Unsplash, Pexels, Shutterstock, etc.) for high-quality, relevant, and properly licensed images.

- Use AI image generation tools (Midjourney, Leonardo.AI, DALL-E, Flux, or similar) to create custom hero images, product mockups, backgrounds, lifestyle scenes, and graphics that match our brand.

- Use design tools (Photoshop, Figma, Canva, or any professional design software) to create/edit banners, infographics, icons, buttons, custom illustrations, and overall page aesthetics.

- Ensure every funnel is on-brand, mobile-responsive, fast-loading, and built for maximum conversions in the supplement space.

- Set up and manage our Checkout Champ account from the ground up (including structure, integrations, tracking, and testing environments) if needed.

- Continuously build, test, iterate, and optimize new funnels as we scale products and offers.

- Deliver work quickly while maintaining extremely high standards of quality and attention to detail.



Requirements



- Deep, proven expertise in Checkout Champ (this is non-negotiable — you must be an advanced user who has built multiple complex, high-converting funnels on the platform).

- Strong visual design sense and the ability to independently conceptualize imagery and overall aesthetics that convert, while still staying true to provided inspiration pieces and brand guidelines.

- Proficiency with AI image generation tools and modern design software (Photoshop, Figma, Canva, etc.).

- Experience sourcing professional imagery online and creating custom visuals with AI to produce scroll-stopping, brand-aligned funnels.

- Fast turnaround — we move quickly and need someone who matches that pace without sacrificing quality.

- Strong portfolio of Checkout Champ funnels you have personally built (please include live links, screenshots, or video walkthroughs in your application — applications without examples will not be considered).

- Excellent attention to detail and the ability to deliver pixel-perfect, professional work.

- Reliable, communicative, and available for consistent, long-term collaboration (this is an ongoing role, not a one-off gig).



If you know Checkout Champ inside and out, love using AI tools and design software creatively, and can take a copy document + inspiration pieces and turn them into beautiful, high-converting funnels on your own, we want to hear from you.



Please apply with:



- A brief overview of your experience with Checkout Champ:

- 2–3 examples of funnels you have built (live links or detailed screenshots preferred)

- Any examples of AI-generated imagery or design work you’ve created for previous projects



We’re excited to find the right long-term partner who can help us scale with stunning, high-performing Checkout Champ funnels.

title: Personal Assistant to Me - Social Media Management & Admin
description:
A bit about me



I'm the founder of a start-up marketing agency and I'm looking for a personal assistant to work directly with me. You won't be dealing with a big corporate structure — it'll just be me and you, and a team of creators we work with. I'll be with you every step of the way, so you'll never be left figuring things out alone. This starts as a light role — around 1 hour a day — but as my agency grows, so do your hours, your responsibilities, and your pay.



What you'll be doing



Manually post to my social media accounts every day (10–15 min per account, starting with 2 — with plans to scale as we grow)



Work alongside the creators I manage — helping to keep things organised and running smoothly on their side too



Keep my spreadsheets accurate and up to date



Calculate creator payouts every week and maintain a clear calculation sheet



Help me with personal tasks and general day-to-day things as they come up



Availability & what you'll need



To start, I'm asking for around 1 hour a day — enough to get into the routine, get comfortable with everything, and build from there. Posting is done manually so I need someone who is available most days. A laptop is ideal, but a tablet or iPad works fine when you're travelling. You just need something to post from.



Days off are completely negotiable — I'm not strict about which days you take, as long as we agree in advance. Life happens and I get that. What I do need is someone I can count on consistently, and who will always let me know ahead of time if they need a day off.



Who I'm looking for



Someone who genuinely enjoys being helpful — not just ticking tasks off a list, but someone who takes pride in making the person they work for's life easier and less stressful



Passionate about building a real working relationship — I want someone who cares about the people they work with, not just the job itself



Someone who communicates well — I want to hear from you, not chase you



Not shy — I want you to speak up, share ideas, and be honest with me



Good with people — you'll be interacting with me and the creators I work with



Reliable — daily posting is the core of this role, I need someone I can genuinely depend on



Open to growing — this is just the start, and I want someone who wants to build something with me



Pay, hours & progression



Starting at $2/hr for roughly 1 hour a day, paid weekly. This is intentionally light to begin with — I want you to settle in and get comfortable before we build on it. As we scale, take on more accounts, and grow our responsibilities together, hours increase and pay rises to $3/hr. I want this to grow into something worthwhile for both of us.



If this sounds like something you'd be great at, I'd love to hear from you. When you apply, tell me a little about yourself — not just your experience, but who you are as a person. That matters a lot to me.

title: Business Development Manager

title: Music Fulfillment Specialist (Full-Time, Full Remote)
description:
Role: Music Fulfillment Specialist (Music & Playlist Curation)

Location: Remote

Schedule: Full-Time | 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EDT (Toronto, Canada Time) 



Role Overview



We’re looking for someone with a strong foundation in music. Not just as a listener, but with real understanding of how music works across production, culture, and the business side of the industry.



As a Fulfillment Specialist, you’ll be responsible for executing music promotion campaigns by placing songs into the right environments. This requires more than basic genre knowledge. You should understand how a track is made, where it fits culturally, and how it can perform in a promotional setting.



A key part of this role is the ability to critically evaluate music, to confidently determine whether a track is strong, average, or below standard, and adjust placement decisions accordingly. This judgment plays a direct role in maintaining the overall quality of campaigns.



What You’ll Be Doing

- Execute music promotion campaigns with accuracy and consistency

- Analyze tracks based on sound, production quality, and overall vibe

- Identify the right genre, subgenre, and audience for each song

- Evaluate and rate music quality to guide placement decisions

- Place songs in environments where they make sense both creatively and commercially

- Manage campaigns while maintaining organization and attention to detail

- Adjust or improve campaigns when needed

- Collaborate with the team to ensure smooth and consistent execution



What We’re Looking For

- Background in music production, audio, or business

- Strong understanding of music culture, scenes, and evolving trends

- Awareness of the music business (promotion, audience targeting, growth strategies)

- Ability to critically assess and rate music quality with confidence

- Strong ear for detail (mixing, arrangement, originality, overall execution)

- Good taste and instinct when it comes to placement decisions

- Detail-oriented, reliable, and consistent in execution



Performance Expectations

- Quality with quantity: placements should be intentional and make sense for the track

- Maintain a consistent and reliable workflow

- Apply both creative judgment and strategic thinking in decisions

- Use music quality assessment to guide placement levels and expectations

- Give additional attention to higher-value or long-term campaigns

- Deliver accurate work with minimal errors



How Success Is Measured

- Strength and relevance of placements

- Consistency in meeting turnaround expectations

- Ability to accurately judge and rate music quality

- Reliability and attention to detail

- Contribution to an organized and effective workflow



Tools & Workflow

- Internal campaign and fulfillment platforms

- Streaming platforms for music review

- Tracking systems and internal documentation tools

- Team communication platforms



Success in This Role Looks Like

- You can quickly determine if a track is high-quality, average, or needs improvement

- Your placements reflect both taste and informed judgment

- You consistently deliver quality work across all campaigns

- You bring insight from music production, culture, and business into your decisions

- You become a trusted voice in evaluating and executing campaigns



Who This Is Perfect For

- Music producers, engineers, or artists

- Playlist curators or A&Rs

- People deeply tapped into music scenes and trends

- Anyone confident in their ability to judge what makes a song good or not

- Individuals who understand both the creative and business side of music

title: AI Video Editor For Ecommerce Company
description:

What you'll be doing:



You'll produce short-form and long-form video content built entirely around AI-generated assets. That means working with AI video generators, AI video tools, AI voice cloning, and whatever new tool drops next week.

Speed — you can turn around content without overthinking it

Details:

Long-term position for the right person

title: eCommerce Graphic Designer (Application Form in Job Description)
description:
## Graphic Designer — Social Media & Ad Creatives



We're looking for a graphic designer with a sharp eye for design, strong DTC e-commerce experience, and the versatility to move between paid social, organic content, and occasional brand work. Most importantly, we want someone who's excited about helping women across Ireland take better care of themselves — through products that genuinely work.



## Who We Are



We're one of Ireland's leading women's health supplement brands. Our hero product is a clinically-backed collagen supplement that helps women in their 40s and beyond support their joints, hair, skin, and overall confidence as they navigate aging, perimenopause, and menopause. We're science-backed but our voice is warm, witty, and real — never clinical, never aspirational. (Brand name shared once we move forward.)



## How This Role Fits



We already have a graphic designer on the team, but she's focused on
 and running at capacity. We're looking to add one more designer to focus on social media creatives — both paid ads and organic — alongside occasional brand work. You'll work with our copy team, video editors, and growth lead to make sure every piece of creative we put out looks as good as the product behind it.



## What You'll Do



- **Paid social ad creatives** — Static image ads for Meta. You'll work directly with our Head of Growth on concepts, formats, and iteration based on what's performing.

- **Organic social content** — Feed posts, stories, and campaign visuals across Instagram,
, and TikTok. You'll work primarily with the Irish marketing team on this.

- **Brand collateral** — Occasional flyers, brochures, packaging touch-ups, event materials, and other one-off design needs.

- **Design system upkeep** — Keep our visual language consistent across everything we put out. Help us grow and refine the brand kit as we scale into new markets (UK and EU expansion is in motion).



A rough baseline is **15–20 polished design pieces per week** — but this number flexes a lot depending on the tools you're set up with (AI, templates, automation) and the priorities of the week. Our goal is to figure out together what your real capacity looks like and equip you to maximize it.



## Where This Could Go



We're in a growth phase. As the design function expands, there's a clear path for the right person to take more ownership over the brand visual direction and eventually lead a small design team. If that excites you, we want to hear about it.



## What You Need to Bring



- **3–5 years of e-commerce / DTC graphic design experience** — ideally with brands that run paid Meta ads at meaningful scale.

- **Strong eye for design.** This is the non-negotiable. Your portfolio should show real polish — clean type, considered composition, brand-appropriate visual decisions. Beautiful work matters more than tool fluency.

- **Photoshop and Illustrator proficiency.** Canva is fine to use, but Canva-only designers won't be a fit — we need someone who can handle more complex execution when needed.

- **A portfolio with DTC ad creatives and social feed work.** If you've worked with women's health, wellness, beauty, or women-focused brands, that's a significant plus.

- **Strong communication in English** (written and verbal).

- **Reliable internet, your own equipment, and a quiet workspace** for occasional video calls.



Nice to have (not required):

- Product photography editing / retouching

- Experience designing for European markets



## How We Work



- **Remote, async-friendly.** We don't track hours or require time-in/time-out. We do expect you to be responsive during Philippine working hours (9 AM – 6 PM PHT) for questions, feedback, and quick async collaboration.

- **Occasional after-hours meetings.** Because of the Irish time zone, some calls happen between 6–8 PM PHT. These are scheduled in advance, never last-minute.

- **You'll work primarily with our Irish marketing team** on organic and brand work, and with our Philippines-based Head of Growth on paid ads.

- **Brand systems exist** — we have a brand kit, ad templates, and a clear visual language. But we want someone who'll help us evolve and strengthen those systems, not just execute against them.



## Compensation & Setup



- **$600–$700 USD per month**, depending on experience.

- **Full-time contract role**, paid monthly via Wise directly to you.

- **Remote, work from anywhere** (Philippines preferred for time zone).

- This is a contractor role. You're responsible for your own taxes and government benefits (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG if applicable).



## Hiring Process



1. **Application** — Fill out the form linked below.

2. **Initial review** — We'll review your portfolio and answers within a few days.

3. **Interview or trial** — Depending on the strength of the application, you'll either go to a short interview first or straight to a quick trial project. The trial is unpaid and small in scope — designed for us to assess your work, and just as importantly, for you to assess the kind of work you'd be doing with us.

4. **Offer** — If both sides feel it's a fit, we move to onboarding.



## Why This Role Matters



Every ad we run, every social post, every visual that lands in front of an Irish woman thinking about her health is a chance to earn her trust. Great design is what makes that chance count. If you want to do work that's seen by a real audience, that drives real results, and that helps real women feel better in their own skin — we'd love to hear from you.



DO NOT
 TO APPLY, fill out the application form below to submit your application:

title: Phone Sales & Events Specialist (Eventbrite + Sales + Customer Experience)
description:
## About Us



We are a fast-growing events company based in Washington, D.C., hosting high-energy singles mixers, speed dating events, and social experiences across Northern Virginia and the DMV area.



We run dozens of events every week and manage 100+ live Eventbrite listings. Our focus is simple: drive attendance, create great experiences, and generate strong revenue through sales and upselling.



We are looking for driven, confident Phone Sales & Events Specialists who can convert leads into paid attendees, support event operations, and deliver excellent customer experiences.



## Role Overview



This is a sales-first role.



You will be responsible for:



* Calling leads and converting them into ticket buyers

* Upselling customers into higher-tier packages (VIP, Elite, Concierge)

* Assisting with event setup, coordination, and attendee management

* Managing Eventbrite listings and customer communication



If you are comfortable on the phone, persuasive, and know how to close, this role is for you.



## Work Schedule



* Flexible hours (must overlap with U.S. EST time)

* Availability during evenings and weekends is a big plus (peak sales and event times)



## Compensation



* $2 – $5 USD per hour (based on experience) Plus Commission. 

* Plus performance bonuses for:



  * Ticket sales

  * Upsells

  * Revenue generated



High performers will earn more and grow quickly.



## Key Responsibilities



### Phone Sales & Lead Conversion



* Call inbound and outbound leads

* Convert inquiries into paid event attendees

* Follow up with past attendees and warm leads

* Handle objections and close sales confidently



### Upselling & Revenue Growth



* Promote VIP, Elite, and Concierge ticket tiers

* Increase average order value per customer

* Recommend the best event experiences based on customer needs



### Eventbrite & Event Management



* Create and update Eventbrite listings

* Monitor ticket sales and optimize listings

* Duplicate and manage multiple weekly events



### Customer Support



* Respond to
s and inquiries quickly

* Provide clear, professional communication

* Handle refund requests and customer concerns



### Event Coordination Support



* Assist with organizing events

* Coordinate with venues and reservations

* Help ensure smooth event execution



## Requirements



* Strong spoken and written English

* Proven experience in phone sales or closing deals

* Experience with Eventbrite, event management, or marketing

* Comfortable upselling and handling objections

* Strong customer service skills

* Organized and detail-oriented

* Reliable internet and ability to work independently



## Do NOT Apply If



* You are not comfortable making phone calls

* You cannot sell or upsell confidently

* You miss deadlines or respond slowly

* You are not serious about performance and results



## Bonus Skills (Highly Preferred)



* Event hosting or event coordination experience

* CRM or lead management experience

* Sales scripts and funnel experience

* Experience working with U.S.-based customers



## What We’re Looking For



We want someone who:



* Knows how to sell and close deals

* Is proactive and follows up without being told

* Thinks in terms of revenue and results

* Communicates clearly and professionally

* Wants to grow with a fast-scaling company



This role can grow into:



* Sales Manager

* Event Manager

* Operations Lead



## How to Apply



To apply:



1.Send your resume and response here through ONJ



2. Include:



* Your phone sales experience

* Your event or Eventbrite experience

* A short example of how you closed a sale

* Your availability (EST time)



3. Attach your resume



## Hiring Timeline



We are hiring immediately and will make decisions within 48 hours after interviews.





If you are confident on the phone, hungry to sell, and want to grow with a fast-moving events company, we want you on our team.

title: Remote Sales & Customer Conversion Agent (Salary-Based + Target-Driven)
description:
Role Overview

We are hiring a Remote Sales & Customer Conversion Agent whose primary responsibility is to convert incoming leads into paying customers and consistently meet monthly sales targets.

This is a strictly sales-focused role — not customer support.

- Your job is to sell flight tickets and close deals

- You are expected to turn conversations into successful bookings



Lead System (Important)

* Skavala will provide daily incoming leads from
 & Instagram ads

* You will be responsible for converting these leads into paying customers

???? In addition, you are also expected to source your own customers independently to ensure you meet your monthly targets

?? This is a standard expectation — not optional?

?? No additional commission is provided for personal leads?

???? It is part of your responsibility to meet your required performance



Key Responsibilities

* Respond quickly to incoming leads (WhatsApp, chat, etc.)

* Engage customers and understand their travel needs

* Recommend suitable flight options

* Follow up consistently until the customer is ready to pay

* Handle objections and close sales confidently

* Convert chats into successful bookings

* Source additional customers independently when needed

* Maintain strong daily and monthly performance



?? Important Rule (Strict)

This role is 100% SALES-DRIVEN.

* You are not hired to chat

* You are hired to convert and close deals

If you cannot consistently turn conversations into payments, this role will not be a good fit.



Salary & Performance Structure

* Base Salary: $300/month

* Salary is tied to performance and target achievement

* You must meet your monthly booking target to maintain this role

* Consistent underperformance may lead to termination



Monthly Target Requirement

* You are required to achieve a minimum number of flight bookings per month

* Targets are mandatory and non-negotiable

* Monthly targets may be increased over time based on performance and business growth



What We Provide

* Daily incoming leads from paid ads

* Structured sales environment

* Opportunity to work with a global travel brand

* Long-term growth for high-performing agents



Requirements

* Proven experience in sales, closing, or customer conversion

* Strong persuasion and negotiation skills

* Ability to confidently handle objections and close deals

* Fast response time and high availability

* Self-motivated and target-driven mindset

* Ability to work independently and take responsibility for results

* Experience in travel or ticket booking is an added advantage



Ideal Candidate

* You are a closer, not a support agent

* You are driven by targets and results

* You can handle pressure and still deliver

* You are proactive and can generate customers when needed

* You take full responsibility for your sales performance



How to Apply

Please include the following in your application:

1. Your experience in sales or closing deals

2. Examples of how you converted leads into paying customers

3. Your strategy for turning chats into sales

(Include the word "Sharp Mind" in your responds to the job offer)

4. Your daily availability



Final Note

This is a high-performance sales role.

???? You will be given leads?

???? You are expected to convert?

???? You must meet targets

If you are confident in your ability to sell, close deals, and deliver results consistently, we want you on our team.

If you are only looking for a support/chat job, please do not apply.

description:

title: Virtual Sales & Admin Representative
description:
About the Role



We’re a residential window cleaning company in Oahu looking for a reliable, organized, phone?savvy virtual assistant to handle sales, customer service, and admin. Must work 9:00 am – 5:00 pm, Monday–Friday (Hawaii–Aleutian Standard Time)



Your mission is simple: Keep the schedule full, customers happy, and the back office organized.



---



Key Responsibilities



1. Sales (Primary)



- Respond quickly to new lead inquiries by phone, text, and
.  

- Provide basic job estimates using our pricing tools and guidelines.  

- Work the sales pipelines daily: move leads through each stage and prevent drop?offs.  

- Run 7?day follow?up cadences with open estimates and unbooked leads.  

- Pull jobs forward to fill gaps in the schedule by contacting flexible customers.  

- Update CRM notes daily so every lead and client has clear, current information.





2. Customer Service (CSR)



- Monitor and protect customer satisfaction on every job.  

- Handle complaints professionally; document issues and schedule go backs (touch ups)  

- Support technicians with on?job issues (access, notes, rescheduling).  

- Send and manage booking confirmations and reminders.  

- Answer service questions about pricing, services, and availability.  

- Process reschedules/cancellations, and backfill those openings from the pipeline.  

- Monitor review responses and follow up with happy customers to get Google reviews.  

- Identify and plan simple upsell opportunities (add?on services, maintenance plans).



3. Admin



- Track bonuses and commissions based on completed jobs and upsells.  

- Send internal updates and reminders to the field team (special instructions, notes).  

- Plan and document yard sign routes for technicians.  

- Process refunds once approved and record them properly.  

- Set up and track client payment plans when needed.  

- Monitor overdue invoices and run polite follow?up calls/texts; log all attempts.



---



Requirements



- 1+ year experience in phone?based customer service, inside sales, or appointment setting (home services a plus).  

- Strong spoken and written English; confident on the phone and in text/email.  

- Comfortable making and receiving a high volume of calls and messages.  

- Highly organized, detail?oriented, and able to follow scripts and checklists.  

- Experience with CRM (HouseCall Pro) and basic office tools 
, calendars, spreadsheets).  

- Reliable internet and quiet workspace.  

- Able to work 9:00 am – 5:00 pm, Monday–Friday, Hawaii–Aleutian Standard Time consistently.



---



Compensation



- Hourly base pay starting at $7/hour with growth opportunities 

- Performance bonuses tied to:  

  - revenue sold and collected

  - service plans sold



You’ll get training on our systems, scripts, and processes. Your work will directly impact how quickly we grow and how well we serve our customers.

title: YouTube Video Editor — Finance / Personal Finance Channel (Long-Form, 10–14 min)
description:
I am CJ Wallace, founder of The Velocity Channel — a personal finance YouTube channel focused on Velocity Banking, debt elimination, and financial freedom strategies. We currently have 64,000+ subscribers and are growing fast. I need a dedicated video editor who will become the backbone of this channel's content production.

This is a long-term position for someone who wants to grow with a brand, not just complete one-off projects. If you are detail-oriented, fast, and take pride in your work, I want to hear from you.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:



Edit 10–12 long-form YouTube videos per month (average 12 minutes each)

Cut and structure raw talking-head footage into clean, engaging final videos

Add lower thirds, text overlays, and on-screen graphics consistent with our brand (navy blue #0D1B2A and gold #C9A84C)

Sync b-roll, whiteboard footage, and supporting visuals to the spoken content

Add royalty-free background music at appropriate levels

Create or place YouTube thumbnail graphics (template will be provided)

Export videos in the correct YouTube format and upload-ready specs

Incorporate feedback efficiently — we aim for no more than one revision round per video

Maintain consistent pacing and style across all videos so the channel feels cohesive





WHO YOU ARE:



Experienced editing talking-head / educational YouTube content (finance, coaching, or teaching channels preferred)

Proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve

Strong eye for pacing — you know when to cut, when to hold, and how to keep a viewer engaged

Detail-oriented — you catch your own mistakes before I do

Reliable communicator — you respond within 24 hours and meet deadlines consistently

Able to take creative direction and follow a brand style guide

Long-term minded — I am not looking for someone to do one video and disappear





REQUIREMENTS:



Minimum 1 year of video editing experience for YouTube channels

Portfolio or sample edits required to apply (links only — no attachments)

Strong written and spoken English

Reliable internet connection and computer capable of handling 1080p video

Available during overlap hours with US Central Time (CST) for communication

Familiarity with personal finance, debt, or financial education content is a strong plus but not required





WHAT I PROVIDE:



Raw footage delivered via Google Drive after each filming session

Brand style guide with colors, fonts, logo files, and examples of the look I want

Script documents for reference so you know the intended flow of each video

Clear feedback — I will tell you exactly what I want changed and why

Consistent monthly volume — this is not sporadic work, it is a reliable schedule

Room to grow — as the channel grows, your role can grow with it





HOW TO APPLY:

To be considered, your application must include all three of the following:



A link to at least two YouTube videos you have edited (must be long-form, 8 minutes or longer)

Your monthly rate expectation

A one-paragraph answer to this question: "What do you do in the first 30 seconds of an edit to make sure a viewer does not click away?"



Applications without all three items will not be reviewed.



SKILLS TAGS TO SELECT ON THE FORM:

Video Editing, Adobe Premiere Pro, YouTube, Video Production, DaVinci Resolve, Graphic Design (basic), English Speaking

title: Client Success Representative (Sales Support) Swing Supplies | Dallas, TX | Remote | Full-Time
description:
About Swing Supplies

Swing Supplies is a fast-growing custom golf merchandise company based in Dallas, TX. We design and deliver premium custom headcovers, towels, and branded gear for golf tournaments, charity events, school programs, and corporate outings across the US. We're in our systemization year, building the team and processes that will carry us into 2027 and beyond.



About the Role

We're looking for a Client Success Representative who is warm, sharp, and genuinely loves people. You'll be the first point of contact for inbound leads - responding quickly, building relationships, and guiding clients from first inquiry all the way to a closed order.

This role works closely with our Sales Manager and Operations team. If you're someone who communicates naturally, follows up without being pushed, and takes pride in making clients feel genuinely taken care of, this is for you.



What You'll Be Doing



Responding to inbound leads via
, CRM, and messaging platforms

Sending quotes, following up consistently, and nurturing relationships at every stage

Qualifying leads based on order quantity and event fit

Drafting personalized client messages that feel warm and human — not scripted

Coordinating with operations to keep clients updated on their order status

Updating and maintaining the CRM (HoneyBook) and lead tracker accurately every day

Supporting the Sales Manager on pricing conversations, proposals, and special requests

Representing the Swing Supplies brand with care and professionalism in every interaction





What We're Looking For



Strong written and verbal English communication — you write like a real person, not a template

Able to build trust quickly and maintain long-term relationships, not just close one-off transactions

Detail-oriented with client requests, designs, quantities, and deadlines

Comfortable managing multiple conversations simultaneously without dropping the ball

Responsive and reliable - you have a natural sense of urgency

Self-sufficient and proactive - you move conversations forward without constant direction

Able to handle objections around pricing, minimums, and timelines with confidence and composure

Warm and professional - you keep communication human even when things get busy

Coachable and open to feedback while staying consistent in quality

Thinks in terms of long-term client relationships, not just transactions

1–3 years of experience in sales support, customer service, or client-facing roles preferred





Bonus Points



Familiar with HoneyBook, invoicing tools, or quoting platforms

Background in events, tournaments, school programs, or branded merchandise

Experience in golf, sports, or custom products industry





What You Get



Remote, flexible work setup

Direct mentorship from a hands-on Sales Manager

A tight-knit, collaborative team that genuinely invests in your growth

Front-row seat to a growing company — real opportunity to grow with us into 2027





How to Apply

Please fill out our application form here: 

The form includes a short video introduction.  We want to hear your voice, see your personality, and get a feel for how you communicate. Don't overthink it,  just be yourself!

title: Transaction Coordinator ! REVA! Needed Asap
description:
Job Title: Transaction Coordinator (Real Estate Investing & Wholesaling)



Location: Remote

Type: Full-Time 



~ About the Role ~



We are a fast-growing real estate investment company seeking an experienced Transaction Coordinator with a strong background in wholesaling, investment properties, and title coordination. This role is critical in ensuring smooth, timely closings by managing contracts, communicating with title companies, and clearing title issues efficiently.



If you understand investment deals, assignments, double closes, and how to keep transactions moving from contract to close, we want to talk to you.



~ Key Responsibilities ~ 

* Coordinate real estate transactions from executed contract through closing

* Manage wholesaling transactions, including assignment contracts and double closes

* Communicate directly with title companies, escrow officers, buyers, and sellers

* Order title, review title commitments, and work with title companies to clear title issues

* Track critical dates, deadlines, and closing timelines

* Ensure all documents are properly executed and submitted

* Maintain organized transaction files and update CRM or tracking systems

* Proactively identify and resolve issues that could delay closing



~ Required Experience & Qualifications ~

* Proven experience as a Transaction Coordinator in real estate investing or wholesaling

* Strong understanding of investment property transactions

* Hands-on experience working with title companies and clearing title

* Familiarity with liens, judgments, probate, code violations, and other title issues

* Experience with assignment agreements and wholesaling workflows

* Excellent communication and follow-up skills

* Highly organized and detail-oriented

* Ability to manage multiple transactions at once and meet deadlines



~ Preferred (Nice to Have) ~

* Experience with CRM systems (Podio, InvestorFuse, REsimpli, etc.)

* Knowledge of real estate contracts across multiple states

* Experience working with cash buyers and private investors



~ What We Offer ~

* Competitive pay (based on experience)

* Consistent deal flow

* Flexible work environment

* Opportunity to grow with a scaling real estate investment company+



~ How to Apply ~

* Please submit your resume and briefly describe your experience with:

* Wholesaling transactions

* Working with title companies

* Clearing title issues

title: Dispute manager
description:
About the Role

We are looking for an experienced Dispute Manager to handle Shopify Payments chargebacks and disputes on our behalf. You will own the full dispute process — from reviewing cases to submitting evidence — and help us recover revenue while keeping our chargeback ratio low.



Responsibilities

Handle disputes in Shopify Payments dashboard

Respond to all dispute types: Item Not Received (INR), Item Significantly Not as Described (SNAD), Unauthorized Transactions, Billing Errors, Duplicate Payments, and Canceled Subscriptions

Collect and submit evidence packages within platform deadlines

Track case outcomes and report on win rate, volume, and recovered amounts

Flag patterns or recurring issues that may indicate fraud or process gaps



Requirements

Proven experience handling Shopify Payments chargebacks (required)

Must include dispute win rate from previous role(s) in your application

Familiarity with all common dispute types and evidence best practices

Strong written English for crafting dispute responses

Able to work independently and meet strict deadlines

Experience in the  Fashion dropshipping niche



Compensation

$6 per hour 

Volume bonuses possible for consistently high win rates



How to Apply

Send a short application

Your experience with Shopify Payments disputes for fashion dropshipping stores

Your average dispute win rate from previous work

Any tools or dashboards you've used for dispute tracking

title: SEO Product Content Assistant (Part-Time)
description:
We are a fast-growing UK women's fashion brand selling on Shopify with Google Shopping ads. We have 1,000+ products and are looking for a detail-oriented assistant to help optimise our product pages for SEO and Google Shopping performance.



You'll work directly with an AI tool (Claude) to audit and rewrite product titles, meta descriptions, product descriptions, image alt texts and URL slugs across our entire catalogue. No prior fashion experience is required - full training and a clear process will be provided from day one.



What You'll Do:

- Review existing product pages on our Shopify store

- Work with Claude (AI) to generate optimised titles, descriptions and alt texts for each product

- Copy and paste optimised content accurately into Shopify's product editor

- Update image alt texts one by one across each product

- Flag products with issues such as AI-generated images, factual errors or missing information

- Work through a prioritised list of products systematically, tracking progress as you go



What a Typical Session Looks Like: 0pen a product in Shopify, share the URL or screenshot with Claude, receive the full SEO optimisation, then apply it directly into Shopify. Repeat. It's methodical, focused work with clear outputs every session.

title: AI Product Image Creator (Part-Time)
description:
We are a fast-growing women's fashion brand with 1,000+ products on Shopify. We are looking for a creative and technically skilled individual to improve and expand our product image library using AI image generation tools.



You'll take existing product images from our Shopify store and use AI tools to create enhanced, additional and lifestyle-style images that better showcase our products.



This is not a photography role. It is an AI-assisted creative role that sits at the intersection of fashion styling, visual merchandising and emerging AI image technology.



What You'll Do:

- Review existing product images across our Shopify catalogue

- Use AI image generation tools (such as Midjourney, Adobe Firefly, Stable Diffusion or similar) to create new and improved product images

- Generate lifestyle shots showing products styled and worn in context — replacing plain studio shots where possible

- Ensure all generated images accurately represent the product — correct colour, fabric appearance, silhouette and styling details

- Create multiple angles and styled views where only one image currently exists

- Replace any AI-generated placeholder images that don't accurately represent the product (we currently have some of these and they need urgent attention)

- Upload completed images to the correct product in Shopify and ensure they are ordered correctly



What Good Output Looks Like:

A product that currently has 2-3 plain flat-lay images should end up with 6–8 images including front view, back view, close-up detail shot, and at least one lifestyle/styled shot showing the garment worn in a realistic setting.

title: Job Title: Creative Content Creator – Gift Wrapping Specialist (Reels & TikTok)
description:
AUGC Content Creator – Gift Wrapping & Satisfying Reels (Ongoing)



Are you obsessed with perfect creases, beautiful ribbons, and satisfying gift-wrapping videos? We are looking for a talented content creator to produce clean, aesthetic, and satisfying wrapping videos for social media. This is an ongoing role for someone who enjoys creating visually pleasing, detail-focused content.



We are inspired by high-end wrapping content such 
 
 
 will create content with a similar feel and aesthetic while bringing your own creative touch.



Style requirements: faceless (hands only), no voiceover required, clean and uncluttered background, bright natural or soft lighting, professional camera angles such as top-down and close-ups, strong focus on wrapping techniques, folds, ribbons and presentation.



Your role: create 2 fully shot and edited videos per week, film consistent high-quality content in a similar setup each time, edit videos with smooth and satisfying transitions, recreate and interpret popular wrapping styles and techniques.



Requirements: strong eye for detail and aesthetics, experience with short-form video such as TikTok or Reels, basic editing skills using tools like CapCut, VN, Canva or similar, access to a good smartphone camera and a stable tripod or overhead setup.



Compensation: monthly retainer of $120 AUD per month plus $20 AUD supply budget for wrapping materials.



How to apply: send 2 to 3 examples of similar content you have created. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to complete a small paid test video.



We are looking for someone who can nail the "tricky" details of gift wrapping. If your demo is top-quality, we can start immediately.
