title: Executive Virtual Assistant
description:
Location: Remote (Philippines preferred)

Type: Full-time

Reporting to: CEO & Founder



About Us

CyberXcel Training Academy is a premium cybersecurity career training program based in Australia. We help IT professionals transition into high-paying cybersecurity roles through hands-on, industry-grade training. We're a fast-moving, remote-first team and we operate at a high standard.



About the Role

We're looking for a sharp, highly organised Executive VA to be the operational backbone of the business. You'll work directly with the CEO and touch almost every part of the business — from student communications and marketing execution to finance tracking, team coordination, and calendar management. This is not a task-monkey role. We need someone who takes ownership, thinks ahead, and gets things done without being chased.



What You'll Be Doing

Marketing & Social Media



Schedule and publish content across Instagram and other social platforms

Manage and execute
 campaigns via Go High Level

Support social media marketing activities as directed

Coordinate with video editors and content creators — manage assets, timelines, and delivery



CRM & Sales Support



Manage workflows, pipelines, and invoicing inside Go High Level

Send contracts and onboarding documents to new students

Call prospects and confirm upcoming sales call appointments

Coordinate scheduling between the CEO, students, and clients



Student Communications



Liaise with current students on behalf of the CEO for non-delivery matters

Handle student queries, follow-ups, and general communications as directed



Finance & Reporting



Track payments, invoices, commissions, and bills using Google Sheets

Monitor and report on monthly cash in, ad spend, and business expenses

Support invoicing and financial admin tied to student onboarding

Flag discrepancies and keep financial trackers up to date



Team Coordination



Coordinate between the CEO, technical mentor, and recruiter on operational (non-delivery) matters

Assign and follow up on tasks in ClickUp to keep the team on track



Operations & Admin



Manage the CEO's calendar and coordinate appointments

Handle inbox and
 management — flag, filter, and respond where appropriate

Communicate with the internal team via Slack

Conduct personal and business research as required

Handle personal admin tasks as directed by the CEO



Course Platform & Content



Make content updates and basic edits on the Circle course platform

Basic video editing for social media or course content as needed





What We're Looking For



Proven experience as an Executive VA or Senior VA (2+ years minimum)

Strong working knowledge of Go High Level (GHL) — non-negotiable

Proficient in Google Sheets — comfortable building and maintaining trackers and reports

Familiar with Circle, ClickUp, Slack, and Instagram scheduling tools

Excellent written and spoken English

Confident making calls and speaking professionally with prospects, students, and clients

Highly organised, proactive, and able to manage competing priorities without being chased

Discreet and trustworthy — you'll handle sensitive financial, student, and client data

Available to overlap with Australian Eastern Time (AEST) business hours





Bonus Points



Experience working with an online education or coaching business

Basic video editing skills (CapCut, Adobe, or similar)

Background in digital marketing or CRM management



Working Hours



Monday to Friday

9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Manila time (PHT)

This aligns with Australian Eastern business hours and provides overlap with the CEO based in Dubai



How to Apply

Send your CV and a short Loom video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself and walking us through one example of how you've helped a previous employer stay organised or solve an operational problem. Applications without the Loom video will not be considered.

title: Script (Steps) Writer + Screen recorder
description:
Our videos for this role will primarily focus on tutorials, reviews, and step-by-step walkthroughs of SaaS software and digital products.



Your responsibility is to first create a clear, structured set of steps for each video. These steps will then be used to guide your screen recording, where you’ll walk through the software in real time, following the exact flow of the steps.



Here’s an example of how the final step-by-step structure should look:?



You’ll need to break down the entire process into individual steps that will be shown in the video. This includes everything, navigation, buttons clicked, inputs entered, prompts used, and any important on-screen actions. Provide any assets that are generated or relevant to the walkthrough (example: if you generate a video as part of the walkthrough, download the video and provide that with any other assets. Make sure they're labelled to the corresponding step). 



Once completed, send the steps using the same format as the example above. We will then convert your steps into a polished final script for you to use during recording.?Example script:?



After receiving the final script, record your screen while going through the tool or course, following the script exactly — as if you’re performing a live walkthrough.



Requirements:

- Excellent attention to detail, you must follow steps exactly with no missed actions

- Understanding of engaging video flow (you know how to structure steps so they make sense and keep the viewer engaged)

- Experience with screen recording tutorials or SaaS walkthroughs is a plus

- Fluent in English with clear communication skills

- Create a separate Chrome profile named “AFFContent” for all recordings. This ensures no personal bookmarks, tabs, or browsing history appear in the video.

- Ensure your screen recording is high quality, smooth, clear, and free from lag.

- Move at a natural pace: don’t rush through steps, but also avoid unnecessary delays.

- Most recordings should fall within the 10–20 minute range, depending on the length of the script.

- Follow the script from beginning to end in the correct order. This is essential, as the editor will sync your footage with a voiceover.

- Reliable, consistent, and responsive



We will provide access to any paid tools or gated content required for the task. Please use resources efficiently and avoid unnecessary usage of credits & generations, or features.

title: Full-Time Video Editor
description:
Hey — I’m Karos. I’m 17, and I’m running a 7-figure e-commerce brand. This is not a corporate, LinkedIn-style job post. No fluff. No buzzwords. No BS. I’m looking for a full-time Video Editor who understands direct response and actually cares about performance. This is not a “just cut clips together” role. This is about making ads that perform.



I’m building fast and I’m obsessed with results. This is only the beginning. If you join now, you’re not just editing videos — you’re growing with me, long-term. This is NOT a short-term gig. It’s a chance to build something real together.



What you’ll be doing:

Editing direct-response video ads (Meta-focused)

Creating high-converting ads, not just aesthetic edits

Producing 3 hooks per video

Turning our raw ideas + strategy into scroll-stopping creatives

Using AI tools like ElevenLabs and AI Personas where it makes sense

Iterating based on performance data

Working directly with me — fast feedback, fast decisions

Helping push the brand from “doing well” to dominating



VERY Highly valued skill:

- AI: Experience with tools like HeyGen, Google Veo, Kling, Nano Banana

Candidates with this skill set will have a strong advantage.



Who I’m looking for:

You’ve edited direct-response ads for e-commerce brands

You understand performance, not just visuals

You have a STRONG EYE for details

You’re fast, disciplined, and competitive

You care about results and high standards

You want to work exclusively on one brand and help grow it

If you want something slow, safe, and corporate — this isn’t for you.



Compensation:

Full-time, remote role

Base salary depends on experience and skill

Performance-based bonuses tied directly to ad results

For strong performers, monthly bonuses can realistically reach around $1,000, depending on output and performance. There’s no fixed cap — results drive upside. If you make winning ads, you make more money. When the brand wins, you win.



The Big opportunity:

This starts as a Video Editor role, but there’s room to grow. If you consistently deliver, understand what’s working, and show good judgment over time, you can move into a better role with more responsibility and higher pay. I’m building brands for the long run and looking for people who want stability, ownership, and multi-year collaboration, not constant job hopping.



Application process (important):

To filter unserious applicants, start your message with:

“I create high-converting creatives.”

Then include:

Your experience with video editing & direct response ads

Whether you have AI experience

Links to relevant e-commerce ad work (portfolio)

Your availability and start date

Why you want to work with us



If this resonates and you’d thrive in this environment, D
 and I’ll get back to you.

Speak soon, Karos

title: Affiliate Marketing and Social Media Manager
description:
About Trillix

Trillix is a fast-growing crypto gaming brand focused on international markets. We are building a high-performance acquisition engine across affiliates, social media, SEO, partnerships, and online communities. We are looking for our first key hire in the Philippines to help drive real user growth.



Role Summary

We need a highly resourceful, aggressive, and organized Growth & Affiliate Manager who knows how to find opportunities, build relationships, and bring in real traffic.



Your mission is simple:



Find people, platforms, and partners that can send depositing users to Trillix.



This is not a passive admin role. We want someone who can proactively reach out, negotiate deals, test campaigns, and grow traffic every week.



Key Responsibilities

1. Affiliate Recruitment & Management

Find and contact casino, sportsbook, crypto, and gambling affiliates worldwide



Recruit new affiliates through
,
, X, Discord, LinkedIn, forums, and direct outreach



Build and maintain affiliate relationships



Negotiate CPA / Rev Share deals



Track affiliate performance and optimize results



Create weekly reports on traffic, signups, deposits, and ROI



2. Partnership Outreach

Contact review sites, comparison sites, influencers, streamers, and industry contacts



Get Trillix listed on relevant casino review websites



Build partnerships with
 channel owners, YouTubers, and community admins



Find media placements and sponsorship opportunities



3. Social Media Growth

Manage Trillix pages on X, Instagram,
, Discord, TikTok, and other platforms



Create and schedule posts, promotions, campaigns, and engagement content



Run giveaways, bonus campaigns, and audience growth strategies



Reply to messages/comments and build community trust



4. SEO & Traffic Growth

Identify keywords and backlink opportunities



Contact websites for guest posts, listings, mentions, and backlinks



Coordinate blog content ideas and traffic campaigns



Help improve organic search presence in target markets



5. User Acquisition Focus

title: US Real Estate Appraiser (Remote)
description:
Overview

We’re looking for a detail-oriented Remote US Real Estate Appraiser to support property valuation and reporting for a fast-paced real estate operation. This role focuses on analyzing residential and/or commercial properties, conducting market research, and preparing accurate appraisal reports that meet US regulatory standards. You will be responsible for evaluating property values using comparable sales, market trends, and industry tools, while ensuring compliance with appraisal guidelines and client requirements.

The ideal candidate is highly analytical, organized, and comfortable working with data, reports, and valuation software. You should be able to manage multiple appraisal assignments, communicate findings clearly, and maintain accuracy under deadlines. Strong attention to detail and knowledge of US real estate markets are essential for success in this role.

Key Responsibilities:

Conduct property valuations using comparable sales (comps), market data, and appraisal methodologies

Prepare detailed appraisal reports in compliance with USPAP standards

Analyze market trends, neighborhood data, and property conditions

Utilize appraisal and real estate software to support accurate valuations

Communicate findings with clients, lenders, and internal teams

Maintain organized documentation and records for each appraisal

Ensure turnaround times and quality standards are consistently met

Required Skills & Qualifications:

Experience in US real estate appraisal or property valuation

Strong analytical and research skills

Familiarity with US housing markets and valuation methods

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

High attention to detail and accuracy

Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a remote setting

Preferred Tools & Software:

Appraisal platforms such as TOTAL by a la mode or Appraise-It Pro

MLS (Multiple Listing Service) systems for comps and market data

Property data tools like CoreLogic, Zillow, or Redfin

GIS or mapping tools for property analysis

Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets for data tracking and reporting

title: CapCut Android editor
description:
**Job Title:** CapCut (Android) Tutor & Long-Term Video Editor



**Job Description:**

I am looking for an experienced editor to provide a series of 1-on-1 coaching sessions conducted via video call. I am developing a character-based brand and need a patient teacher to help me master the Android version of CapCut.



I would like to book an initial block of four coaching sessions, with the long-term goal of having a reliable expert I can consult for help on future projects as the brand grows.



During our sessions, we will focus on:

* **Timeline Management:** Handling high-quality static images and duration.

* **Audio Branding:** Sourcing and precisely timing background music.

* **Voiceover Work:** Using "Text to Speech" tools and adjusting speed and tone.

* **Visual Polish:** Applying subtle movement and transitions to static scenes.

* **Efficiency:** Setting up workflow templates for consistent content production.



Please let me know your experience with the Android app and your availability for a multi-session coaching arrangement. I am looking for a teacher who is patient with beginners.

description:

 Virtual Assistant (Etsy Traffic Focus) responsible for driving external traffic to an Etsy shop and maintaining a consistent, visually strong social media presence.

Write clear, keyword-based titles (based on Etsy listings)

Instagram (content + brand presence)

1 Reel

1 Carousel

2 Static posts

Marketplace listings

Engagement

~15 minutes per day:

reply to comments and DMs, if the come 

interact with relevant profiles 



Requirements

Strong experience with Pinterest

Self-starter with efficient execution

title: Mail Customer Support Specialist — Shopify Health Brand (Long-Term, Remote)
description:
## About Rekuria



Rekuria is a fast-growing premium health & wellness brand in the DACH market (Germany, Austria, Switzerland). Our flagship product — **Neckify**, an electronic neck massage device — helps thousands of people relieve neck pain naturally, without medication.



Our mission: **free people from chronic neck pain through natural, medication-free self-care.**



We're building a long-term health brand with a strong community, high customer LTV, and a voice customers genuinely trust. That's where **you** come in.



---



## The Role: You Become "Rebecca"



You won't just answer tickets. You'll **become the voice of Rebecca** — Rekuria's dedicated customer care persona. Rebecca is:



- **Medically informed** — speaks with authority about pain and natural relief

- **Warm & empathetic** — customers feel safe and in good hands

- **Educational** — empowers people to take action for themselves

- **Fact-based & compliant** — never over-promises, never makes illegal health claims

- **Alternative-minded** — champion of natural, medication-free relief



We'll give you a full Brand Voice Guide, example responses, SOPs, and direct coaching so you can step into Rebecca's voice quickly and confidently.



---



## Your Mission — Measured by 4 KPIs



1. **Near-zero return rate** — solve problems before they become returns

2. **Near-zero chargeback rate** — spot escalations early, de-escalate fast

3. **High 5-star review volume** — customers review us because they want to

4. **Low ticket volume relative to orders** — build automations, FAQs, and flows that prevent tickets



If you only "answer tickets," you're not the right fit. If you want to **build a customer experience system**, you are.



---



## What You'll Do



- Handle conversations across *
 (Gorgias), Instagram DMs, TikTok DMs, Meta/Facebook, WhatsApp**

- Write in Rebecca's voice: medical, professional, warm, empowering

- Proactively prevent returns and chargebacks

- Build and maintain **SOPs, macros, FAQs, and Klaviyo post-purchase flows**

- Solicit UGC and 5-star reviews at the right 
nts

- Stay compliant with **German health advertising law (HWG), Medical Device Regulation (MDR/MPG), and Meta/Google health policies**

- Document in Notion, record Loom walkthroughs, use Claude and AI tools to work smarter

- Weekly KPI report with improvement proposals



---



## Must-Haves



- **3+ years** E-Commerce customer support experience for Shopify brands

- **Direct experience in Health / Wellness / Medical Device niche** — you understand compliance and what can/cannot be said in health marketing

- **Strong written German** — either native/near-native OR C1+ combined with confident use of DeepL/Claude/AI tools, with proven ability to produce polished German customer communication

- Hands-on experience with **Gorgias, Shopify, and Klaviyo**

- Familiar with **HWG, MPG/MDR, and health ad policies on Meta + Google** (or proven track record of quickly mastering compliance frameworks)

- Comfortable with **Loom, Notion, Zoom, Claude, and modern AI tools**

- Strong writer — can shift tone from clinical to warm in one sentence

- Self-starter — you don't wait to be told what to do

- Stable internet, quiet workspace, reliable hours



## Nice-to-Haves



- Experience preventing chargebacks (Stripe / PayPal disputes)

- Copywriting or UGC-solicitation background

- Team lead experience (you'll onboard a second CS hire within 6–12 months as we grow)



---



## Work Setup



- **Hours:** ~20 hours/week to start (flexible schedule covering DACH business hours)

- **Growth path:** Scales to full-time within 3–6 months based on performance

- **Location:** 100% remote

- **Compensation:** $8–$15/hr to start (negotiable based on experience), growing to $15–$20/hr at full-time

- **Long-term:** We want a multi-year partnership, not a short-term hire. As Rekuria grows, you grow with us — more hours, higher pay, leadership opportunities



---



## How to Apply



Applications that don't follow these steps will be ignored. We're hiring one excellent person, not screening 200 generic applicants.



**Step 1:** Start your application with the word **NECKIFY** on the first line. This is how we know you read the entire post.



**Step 2:** Answer these 3 questions (short, direct, no fluff):



1. Which German/DACH e-commerce brand(s) have you worked for? How long, and what niche?

2. Give one specific example of how you prevented a return or chargeback. Walk us through the situation and what you said.

3. Which tools from our stack have you actively used (not just heard of): Gorgias, Shopify, Klaviyo, Notion, Loom, Claude?



**Step 3:** Record a **30–60 second Loom video** introducing yourself (in English or German, your choice). We want to see your communication style, not your studio lighting.



**Step 4 — German Scenario Test:**



*Dieser Teil ist auf Deutsch — bitte auf Deutsch beantworten.*



**Szenario:** Eine Kundin schreibt nach 3 Tagen: *"Das Gerät hilft nicht, ich habe immer noch Schmerzen. Ich will mein Geld zurück."*



**Aufgabe:** Schreibe in 3–5 Sätzen, wie Rebecca diese Nachricht beantworten würde. Du darfst DeepL, Claude oder andere Tools nutzen — wir wollen sehen, wie dein finales Ergebnis klingt.



---



## What Happens Next



1. We review applications and shortlist the top 3–5 candidates

2. 30-minute paid video interview (Zoom) with the founder

3. Paid trial task: Handle 5 real customer tickets in Rebecca's voice — paid regardless of outcome

4. Offer to the best fit — long-term partnership, clear growth path



---



**We're building something that genuinely helps people live pain-free lives. If that mission resonates with you, and you have the skills above, we want to hear from you.**



— Rekuria

“Hallo, ich bin unsicher, ob das Produkt mir wirklich helfen kann. Ich habe schon vieles ausprobiert und möchte kein weiteres Produkt kaufen, das am Ende nichts bringt. Außerdem bin ich bei Gesundheitsprodukten immer etwas skeptisch.”



We are evaluating:



* empathy

* clarity

* professionalism

* trust-building

* tone

title: Marketing & Operations Assistant
description:
Overview

We are looking for a sharp, detail-oriented assistant to support Mark across marketing operations, SEO execution, project coordination, and day-to-day admin. This is a hands-on role that spans multiple functions - you will not be doing just one thing. The right person is organized, proactive, communicates clearly, and can manage ongoing work across several active projects without dropping the ball.



This role starts part-time but has a clear path to grow for the right candidate. We work fast, we expect follow-through, and we want someone who takes ownership of their tasks.



Key Responsibilities



Project & Task Tracking

? Track all active projects, opportunities, and tasks across daily, weekly, and monthly cycles

? Follow up with team members on deadlines and open items

? Keep ClickUp up to date and flag anything that is slipping

? Proactively remind Mark of pending tasks, upcoming deadlines, and action items



Admin & Coordination

? Manage inbox triage, draft responses, and flag
s that need Mark's attention

? Handle calendar scheduling, meeting coordination, and follow-ups

? Edit and format documents, reports, and client-facing materials

? Support team communication on projects and delivery timelines via Slack



SEO & Content Operations

? Generate metadata (titles, descriptions, slugs) according to our internal setup and guidelines

? Research topics and identify supporting content gaps using Ahrefs/Semrush and AI tools

? Create content outlines for blog posts and service/product pages following our defined process

? Optimize images according to our process (naming, compression, alt text)

? Edit headings and formatting inside Google Docs to produce clean, publish-ready drafts

? Review practice area and service pages to identify internal linking opportunities, then implement links directly in blog content



Research & QA

? Research topics, competitors, and client-relevant information as needed

? QA deliverables for accuracy, formatting, and consistency before they go out

? Proofread content, pages, ads, and documents

? Compile reports and data summaries (training provided)



Required Skills & Experience

? Proficient with AI tools and LLMs - this is a core requirement, not a nice-to-have (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, etc.)

? Background in marketing, SEO, or digital agency work

? Working knowledge of SEO principles and content best practices

? Experience with Ahrefs or Semrush for keyword research and content gap analysis

? Proficient in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail, Calendar)

? Familiar with Google Search Console and Google Analytics

? Experience with Slack for team communication

? Experience with ClickUp or similar project management tools



What We Are Looking For

? Highly detail-oriented - catches mistakes before they become problems

? Punctual and reliable - meets deadlines and responds promptly

? Proactive - does not wait to be told twice; follows up and closes loops

? Strong written and verbal communicator in English

? Comfortable working across multiple projects and priorities in the same day

? Calm under pressure - this is a fast-moving environment

? Willing to learn internal processes and adapt quickly



Nice to Have

? Experience supporting a marketing agency or multi-brand operation

? Familiarity with GEO / AI SEO concepts

? Experience with paid ads (Meta, Google) at an operational level

? Basic understanding of HTML or CMS troubleshooting

title: Outbound Business Development & Deal Sourcing Associate (Healthcare / M&A)
description:
Job Title

Outbound Business Development & Deal Sourcing Associate



Company

SP



Location

Remote, Philippines



Job Type

Full-Time



Compensation

Base salary plus performance bonuses



About the Role

SP is a U.S.-based advisory firm focused on behavioral healthcare, addiction treatment, mental health, healthcare growth services, and M&A-related engagements.



We are looking for a sharp, professional, and highly driven remote team member based in the Philippines to help us generate new business opportunities.



This is not a general virtual assistant role.



This role is for someone who is strong in outbound sales, lead generation, research, and business development. You will help identify business owners who may be interested in selling their companies, operators who may need consulting services, and buyers for active engagements.



You do not need to be a senior M&A professional, but you must be intelligent, organized, resourceful, and comfortable learning business and financial concepts.



What You’ll Be Doing



Research U.S.-based behavioral healthcare companies, founders, owners, executives, and decision-makers

Build targeted prospect lists for outbound campaigns

Identify behavioral health business owners who may be potential sell-side clients

Identify potential clients seeking support with startup development, licensing, accreditation, expansion, and new programs

Source buyers for active healthcare M&A opportunities

Conduct outbound outreach through
, LinkedIn, and other approved channels

Write customized and professional outreach messages

Track leads, follow-ups, notes, and pipeline activity in spreadsheets and CRM systems

Help qualify prospects based on fit, size, ownership profile, and likely interest

Work closely with leadership to improve outreach strategy and business development efforts



What We’re Looking For



Strong written and spoken English

Experience in outbound sales, lead generation, appointment setting, SDR, BDR, or business development

Strong online research skills

High attention to detail

Organized and reliable follow-up

Comfortable using spreadsheets, databases, and CRM tools

Able to communicate professionally with U.S.-based executives and business owners

Self-motivated and able to work independently



Strong Plus Factors



Experience working with U.S. clients

Experience in healthcare, consulting, finance, recruiting, business brokerage, private equity, or M&A-related work

Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Apollo, HubSpot, Instantly, Smartlead, Clay, Close, Pipedrive, or similar tools

Ability to understand basic financial and business concepts

Experience sourcing founders, owners, executives, or investors



This Role Is a Good Fit If You



are strong at research and finding hard-to-reach decision-makers

know how to write outbound messages that sound professional and get replies

can think critically about which leads are worth pursuing

are not just completing tasks, but helping generate real opportunities

want a long-term role with growth potential



This Role Is Not a Good Fit If You



are only looking for admin or general VA work

have no outbound sales or prospecting experience

struggle with written English

need constant supervision to stay organized and productive



Performance Expectations

You will be measured based on the quality of your research, outreach consistency, pipeline organization, responsiveness, and ability to help generate qualified opportunities.



Work Schedule



Full-time

9am-5pm EST US.



How to Apply *IMPORTANT*

To apply, please send the following, if you do not send the following, we understand you did not read this post fully, and we are only hiring those who pay attention to detail:



Your resume *

A short summary of your outbound sales or business development experience

Examples of the types of leads or decision-makers you have sourced before

The tools you have used for prospecting, outreach, and CRM

A short cold
 sample to a U.S. healthcare business owner

Your requested monthly compensation

Include the word Origination at the top of your application



Important

We are looking for quality, professionalism, strong judgment, and long-term fit. Generic applications will not be considered.

title: Dating App lead generation / Airbnb Growth manager VA
description:
Job Title

Salary: $260 USD / Month 16 hours per week + Bonus ($5 per "closed" lead) 

Paid weekly 

The Mission: Help me build a multi-million dollar empire across Property Management and Dating Lead Generation appointment setting. I’m from London, and I’m looking for a partner who wants to learn the "London Way"



high-level English, high-level charisma, and high-level results.



The Schedule (London Time):



Sunday to Thursday: 3 hours/day (Evening shift)



Can be flexible with exact shift hours and days



Total: 16 hours of work per week.



The Role: 50% Appointment setting on Dating apps / 50?min Paperwork  Photo Editing AI



The "Dating Assistant" Role (Primary): You will manage Hinge profiles like a pro and dating apps like a pro You’ll use DeepSeek and other AI tools to screenshot and continue the conversation. A/B test which photos and openers get the best response. We are going to build an automated machine where you, optimize the English, and secure dates.



The "Airbnb Specialist" Role: You’ll learn my systems for Airbnb and 
. You’ll be responsible for uploading new listings and ensuring every guest feels like they’re staying in a 5-star hotel. Using a property channel management tools.



The "Daily Strategist": You are my right hand. You plan my day, use AI to automate your own tasks, and keep me "one step ahead" of everything.



Why You?



You are obsessed with AI. You don’t just use it; you want to see how far it can go.



You have a Sales Mindset. You don't just "message"; you "close." you love earning extra!



You are Very Organized. You hate mess and love systems.



You have Ownership. If an Airbnb listing needs a 5-minute fix on a Saturday, you jump on it because you care about the result.



The "Billion Dollar" Perks:



Direct Coaching: I will personally coach you on high-level British English and AI prompting.



Bonuses: I want you to make money when I make money. Every successful appointment you set comes with a cash bonus.



WORKING STYLE:



The 60-Day Sprint: We’ll use Hubstaff for the first 30–60 days as "training wheels." Once you hit your KPIs (e.g., 3 dates/week, 100?min accuracy), we move to a Results-Based Model. I won't care if you work 1 hour or 3 as long as the results are "billion-dollar level," you have total autonomy and freedom to do your thing.



The Output Agreement: Use AI and automation to your heart's content to speed up your work EXCEPT for sensitive apartment contracts and legal documents. Those tasks must be done manually by you to ensure total privacy and zero errors. So you need to still be able to edit, be good at photoshop / editing manually to an average level.



Mistake Ownership: If a listing error happens, I expect you to jump in for 5–10 mins to fix it, regardless of the hour. Once you master the system, this shouldn't be necessary.







HOW TO APPLY:



Tell me about the hardest project you have done online,



Have you worked in Sales before, or what is the hardest thing you had to sell.

title: Long Form Text to Video Editor
description:
Job Posting: Part
 time - Long-Form Sleep Video Editor (Space & Science)



Hi there! I am looking for a dedicated and reliable video editor to help produce ultra-long-form YouTube Sleep videos(2 to 2.5 hours per video) focused on space and science.



I handle the scripts, voiceovers, and thumbnails myself. Your role is to transform these assets into a cohesive, high-quality visual experience by sourcing and syncing the perfect footage to the narration.



Looking for an editor who is available to start immediately.





Long-term Partnership

This is intended to be a long-term, consistent position. As long as the channel remains monetized, this will be steady work. (Please note that i have no influence on this). 



Furthermore, by outsourcing the editing of this channel, I plan to expand and potentially launch new channels. This could lead to more work for the right editor or their professional network in the future.





The Role & Requirements

This is a high-volume position that requires someone with a genuine interest in space and science. Because of the specific nature of these topics, you should have enough background knowledge to distinguish between different spacecraft, celestial bodies, and scientific concepts.



To be considered for this role, you must meet the following non-negotiable criteria:



• Availability: I am looking for someone who can start right away.



• Capacity: You must be available to handle 6 uploads a week (projects of 2 to 2.5 hours each). I upload every day except Thursday. (around 25 uploads a month).



• Scheduling: We will discuss the editing frequency together, but I want to work ahead so I don't receive the video on the day of the upload itself. Staying ahead is important to account for time zone differences and to ensure a buffer, though we won't work too far in advance so we can still respond to current trends.



• Editing Software: CapCut, my personal instructions and manuals are based on CapCut and Macbook apps.





• Technical Setup: You need a high-end computer and a stable, fast internet connection capable of handling massive downloads and rendering long-form video projects.



• Communication: I value reliability and great communication. I need an editor who is fluent in English and provides consistent updates.



• Style: You must have a keen eye for "documentary-style" aesthetics. I use high-quality stock footage only—no green screens, cheap animations, or subscribe buttons. 



*I do not need fancy transitions or complex edits; the priority is high-quality visual matching to the voiceover and the topic.





Our Workflow:

We will collaborate through a shared Google drive folder and communicate via 
.



1. Preparation: I provide the script, voiceover, and background music in a shared dedicated Google drive folder.



2. Project Setup: You will import these assets into your editing software (preferably CapCut because i use that too, and i show how i want certain things in the workflow).



3. Audio Review: You will check the voiceover for any background sounds/glitches and remove them.



4. AI Storyboarding: Download the fine-tuned voiceover file, convert it into an SRT file, and use AI to generate a storyboard. This will make it much easier and faster to find perfectly matching footage for the entire 2+ hour duration. If you have other ideas on how to do this then we can look into this together.



5. Sourcing & Editing: Based on the storyboard and script, you will source high-quality stock footage (from websites I provide). Clips are usually 5–20 seconds long. Transitions are limited to basic black fades.



6. Delivery: You will export the final video and upload it back to the shared Google Drive folder for me to publish.



Next Steps: How we start

If we d
 to work together, I will send you a detailed onboarding document explaining exactly how I work, including visual examples. I will add you to our shared workspaces. To get started right away i will make sure there are 2 scripts and voiceovers ready for you to get started with so you can start right away. I would like to receive the first video that same day, if that is not possible for you then please don’t respond.



For now, this is a visual example of what i’m looking for in the same niche (not my channel): 
 





IMPORTANT: Not everything needs to be matched 100% since people also watch this to fall asleep to, but when they are talking about voyager 1, show videos about voyager 1, or when its talking about Jupiter so the video stays engaging that’s why its important you have some knowledge about space/spacecrafts. *A lot of the parts of the video will just be general space content so that saves a lot of time, also a lot of those videos will be in the video more than once.





How to Apply:

Please include the following in your application:



• Your relevant experience with this type of long-form content / your portfolio.



• What knowledge you have about the space/science niche.



• How you would handle this specific workflow to save time and how you handle the high-volume schedule.



• Your specific availability.



• Your fees.



Please use the word ‘Mars’ in your opening sentence so I know you have read this listing thoroughly.



Thank you in advance!

Talk soon :)



Best,

Kirstin

title: Meta Paid Ads Buyer
description:
We’re hiring a full-time Media Buyer to own our paid advertising performance across platforms, with a primary focus on Meta 
 & Instagram Ads). This role is full-time and in-house (remote), so please only apply if you’re looking for a long-term permanent position. We're not hiring freelance or agency.



This role is ideal for someone who is data-driven, detail-oriented, and excited to work closely with creatives to improve ad performance through clear insights and testing.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

- Launch, manage, and optimize ad campaigns primarily on Meta Ads. Support campaigns on Google, TikTok, or other platforms where relevant.

- Plan and execute structured tests (angles, creatives, offers, landing page) to improve cost per acquisition and ROAS.

- Manage budgets, audiences, and campaign structures based on performance

- Analyze ad performance to identify winning and underperforming ads

- Communicate clear performance insights to the creative team

- Participate in brainstorming sessions to improve creatives based on data

- Analyze Shopify sales data to identify scaling opportunities, conversion bottlenecks and product-level performance

- Monitor competitors’ ads, offers, and campaigns to identify gaps or opportunities in our market niche.

- Stay up-to-date with DTC industry trends and platform updates to ensure best practices.

- Providing administrative support, as and when needed to the manager.



Requirements

- Full-time, Monday–Friday (9am–5pm PH time)

- Remote position

- Strong comfort working with numbers and data

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; able to interpret data to make informed decisions

- Able to explain performance clearly to non-technical teammates

- Highly organized, self-directed, execution-focused, and able to work independently

- Willing to learn quickly and adapt as the business grows



What We Offer

- Competitive salary

- Performance-based bonuses

- Paid annual leave & public holidays

- Fully remote work

- Training, upskilling, and career growth opportunities

- A team that values ownership, clarity, and results



Application

Please send your application via
 to 
 with the subject line: "Meta Paid Ads Buyer – (Your Name)”. Include your OnlineJobsPH profile link and answers to the questions below in numbered format directly in the
 body:

1) Describe your most recent job: your job overview, and how long did you work there? 

2) What challenges did you face, and why did you leave the job? 

3) Describe one (or more) test/experiment you tried that didn't work as expected? What did you learn, and what would you do differently now? 

4) Submit your portfolio. If you don’t have relevant DTC experience, submit your best work. Strong portfolios outside this role may be considered for other roles.

5) Pick one project from the attached portfolio in question (4) that you are most proud of and answer the questions below. You may pick more than one project.

- What was the project?

- Your contribution?

- Why you approached it this way?

- Results (provide metrics if possible)?



Keywords

 ads,
 ads specialist,
 ads manager,
 advertising, meta ads, meta ads specialist, meta ads manager, meta ads buyer, fb ads manager, media buyer,

title: Google ad specialist
description:
Job Title: Google Ads Specialist (Lead Generation – Construction & Renovation Services | WFH)



We are looking for a Google Ads Specialist to help us generate high-quality leads for our construction and renovation services targeting specific areas such as Miami, Florida and nearby locations.



Responsibilities:

 • Plan, create, and manage Google Ads campaigns focused on lead generation

 • Optimize campaigns to improve cost per lead and conversion rate

 • Conduct keyword research for construction and renovation services

 • Target specific service areas (local targeting in the US)

 • Monitor performance and provide simple reports and insights

 • Continuously test and improve ad performance



Requirements:

 • Proven experience in Google Ads (lead generation campaigns preferred)

 • Experience working with service-based businesses (construction, renovations, home services is a plus)

 • Strong understanding of local targeting and conversion tracking

 • Ability to work independently and deliver results



To Apply, Please Send:

 • Resume or portfolio of past Google Ads campaigns

 • Short explanation of how you will help us scale and generate more leads

title: AI-Assisted Executive VA for UK B2B Agency (Full-Time, Long-Term)
description:
Working hours: 6am - 2pm UK time (1pm - 9pm Philippine Time)



We're a UK-based B2B lead generation agency helping commercial cleaning companies win new contracts. We run a fully AI-powered operation using Claude, ScoreApp, Smartlead, Apollo, and Clay. You won't be doing repetitive admin — AI handles that. You'll be the human who reviews AI outputs, makes judgement calls, and keeps the machine running.



We're small, fast-moving, and building something genuinely new. First VA hire means this role grows into Operations Lead within 12 months for the right person.



WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO:



Inbox and reply management (30%):

- Review Claude-classified
 replies and send pre-drafted responses with 1-2 edits

- Schedule meetings using Calendly/AI scheduler

- Flag edge cases that need human judgement



Scorecard and lead nurture (20%):

- Monitor ScoreApp scorecard completions

- Review AI-generated scorecard reports before they go out

- Follow up manually on high-value prospects



List and data hygiene (15%):

- QA Apollo and Clay enrichment outputs

- Maintain CRM (HubSpot or similar)

- Flag data quality issues



Client reporting (15%):

- Review AI-generated weekly client reports

- Edit for tone and accuracy, send to clients

- Take notes on client calls using AI transcription



Onboarding admin (10%):

- Process new client intake forms

- Send onboarding sequences

- Schedule kickoff calls



Ad hoc (10%):

- Whatever the week needs



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:



Required:

- 2+ years experience as a VA for a Western (UK/US/AU) business

- Excellent written English — you'll be editing AI-generated content that goes to UK business owners

- Comfortable with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar)

- Detail-obsessed — the job is catching what AI misses

- Reliable internet, backup power, quiet workspace

- Available 6am - 2pm UK time consistently



Strongly preferred:

- Experience with B2B sales, lead generation, or outbound marketing

- Familiarity with any of: Smartlead, Instantly, Apollo, Clay, HubSpot, ScoreApp, Calendly

- Previous experience in a role that required judgement calls, not just task execution



HOW TO APPLY:



Send your application with:



1. A 2-minute Loom video introducing yourself and answering: "Tell me about a time you caught an error an AI tool made, and how you handled it."



2. Your CV



3. Answer these three questions: monthly salary expectation USD, when you could start, rate your written English 1-10



4. The word SCORECARD at the top of your reply



Applications without the Loom video will not be reviewed. Applications without the word SCORECARD will be auto-rejected.



HIRING PROCESS:

- Day 1-5: Application review, shortlist of 5-8

- Day 6-8: Paid trial task (60 min real work, paid)

- Day 9-10: 30-minute Zoom interview with top 3

- Day 11-14: Offer and contract



Target start date: 3 May 2026



WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT:

- You're employee #1. Your voice shapes how we build.

- AI does the grunt work. You do the thinking.

- Direct access to the founder. No middle management.

- Performance bonuses after month 3.



Posted by Clean Contracts UK Limited (trading name; legal entity Shroomccino Ltd, company number
, registered in England and Wales)

title: KDP Publishing & Launch VA — ARC, Amazon Ads, Multi-Title Catalog
description:
About the Role

We are a growing self-publishing operation producing non-fiction books across multiple genres and author brands. Our catalog is actively expanding — multiple titles are in production at any given time, each with its own launch sequence, ARC campaign, and marketing strategy.



This role exists to own the operational and marketing layer of our publishing business. You will prepare every title for launch, run ARC campaigns from the ground up, execute our marketing strategy across platforms, and manage the post-publication growth of each title. The work is detail-oriented, process-driven, and genuinely important — reviews and launch execution are the primary levers that determine whether a self-published book succeeds or disappears.



For the right person, this is a long-term role with expanding responsibility across a growing catalog. We have a structured playbook for every task — you will not be figuring out the strategy. You will be executing it.



How We Work — Please Read Before Applying

We operate across multiple author pen names. Each author identity is treated as a completely independent brand. You will never disclose, suggest, or imply that any author name is a pen name or that multiple brands are managed by the same operation.



For security reasons, we have a clear division between what the VA prepares and what the publisher finalizes. Understanding and respecting this boundary is a condition of this role:



What the VA owns:

About the Role

We are a growing self-publishing operation producing non-fiction books across multiple genres and author brands. Our catalog is actively expanding — multiple titles are in production at any given time, each with its own launch sequence, ARC campaign, and marketing strategy.



This role exists to own the operational and marketing layer of our publishing business. You will prepare every title for launch, run ARC campaigns from the ground up, execute our marketing strategy across platforms, and manage the post-publication growth of each title. The work is detail-oriented, process-driven, and genuinely important — reviews and launch execution are the primary levers that determine whether a self-published book succeeds or disappears.



For the right person, this is a long-term role with expanding responsibility across a growing catalog. We have a structured playbook for every task — you will not be figuring out the strategy. You will be executing it.



How We Work — Please Read Before Applying

We operate across multiple author pen names. Each author identity is treated as a completely independent brand. You will never disclose, suggest, or imply that any author name is a pen name or that multiple brands are managed by the same operation.



For security reasons, we have a clear division between what the VA prepares and what the publisher finalizes. Understanding and respecting this boundary is a condition of this role:



What the VA owns:

-Manuscript formatting to KDP specifications

-Cover file preparation and print preview verification

-Full metadata preparation: title, description (HTML), 7 keywords, categories, series info, pricing

-Upload-ready package: a complete checklist with all files and metadata ready to paste

-A+ Content design and copy — prepared in Canva, ready for submission

-Amazon Ads campaign setup and ongoing management via Amazon Ads console (separate login provided)

-All ARC distribution, outreach, social media,
 marketing, and community management



What the publisher owns:

-Final review and approval of all files before upload

-Logging into KDP and uploading files to the dashboard

-Executing the final publish action on KDP

-Login credentials to KDP accounts — these are never shared

-Submitting A+ Content through the KDP dashboard

-Final budget approval and spend cap decisions on ads

-Final approval of
 sequences and any major public-facing content before it goes live



 WHY WE WORK THIS WAY

KDP accounts are linked to real financial and identity information. The publisher retains sole access to the KDP dashboard and handles all final uploads personally. This is non-negotiable and is designed to protect both parties. It is not a reflection of trust — it is a professional security standard we apply regardless of who we work with. If this structure does not suit how you prefer to work, this role is not the right fit.



Your Responsibilities — Phase 1: Launch Preparation

For each new title, you will build and deliver a complete launch-ready package before the publication date. The publisher uploads; you prepare everything they need to do it in under 30 minutes.



Manuscript & Metadata Package

Format the manuscript file to KDP paperback and ebook specifications — correct trim size, margins, fonts, chapter breaks, and TOC

Verify print preview is clean — no margin bleed, no spine text errors, correct page count for cover calculation

Write the book description in KDP-ready HTML format — headers, bold text, bullet points formatted for the Amazon listing

Research and confirm 7 keyword phrases using Publisher Rocket or Amazon autocomplete — documented with rationale

Identify 2 optimal KDP categories — primary and secondary — with competition analysis

Prepare pricing recommendation for ebook and paperback based on competitive research

Compile everything into a one-page Upload Checklist the publisher can work through in sequence



A+ Content Design

Design all A+ Content modules in Canva: brand story, image + text modules, comparison chart

Write copy for each module — consistent with the author brand voice and Amazon's content guidelines

Prepare a step-by-step A+ Content submission guide so the publisher can submit in under 20 minutes

Once live (3–7 days after submission), verify modules display correctly and flag any issues



Author Profile Setup

Amazon Author Central: draft author bio, prepare headshot, compile the profile setup — ready for publisher to claim and activate

Goodreads: set up author profile, add bio and photo, link all titles

BookBub: create author profile, add bio and photo, select relevant categories

StoryGraph: add author profile

Carrd author website: build and publish one-page author site per pseudonym with bio, book cover,
 signup, and Amazon link



Your Responsibilities — Phase 2: ARC Campaign

The ARC campaign begins 8 weeks before launch and is one of the most critical things you will do. Reviews in the first 30 days post-launch determine whether Amazon's algorithm pushes the book or ignores it.



Set up ARC landing page on BookFunnel — watermarked ebook file, sign-up form, automated delivery

Submit title to BookSirens for pre-vetted reviewer outreach

Research and build a targeted list of 50+ ARC candidates: BookTok/Bookstagram creators,
 group members, Reddit community members, bloggers — specific to each title's genre and audience

Execute personalized outreach using provided templates — track all contacts in the ARC tracker spreadsheet

Manage the full ARC pipeline: distribute copies, send welcome
s, mid-campaign check-ins, launch-day review request, and one follow-up nudge

Monitor and log all reviews posted across Amazon, Goodreads, StoryGraph, and BookBub

Report weekly on ARC numbers: distributed, opened, reviewed, pending



Your Responsibilities — Phase 3: Marketing Execution

Amazon Advertising

Launch Sponsored Products auto campaign on publication day — provided ad console login, $5–10/day starting budget

After 2 weeks, pull search term report and build manual keyword and ASIN-targeting campaigns

Monitor ACOS weekly — pause underperforming targets, scale winners, report results every Monday

Submit BookBub Featured Deal applications once each title reaches 10+ reviews



Promotional Campaigns

Schedule and submit titles to Freebooksy, BargainBooksy, and ManyBooks during KDP Free and Countdown Deal windows

Coordinate KDP promotional pricing windows — prepare the pricing change schedule and submit for publisher approval before execution

Pitch to relevant Substack newsletters and homesteading/parenting/self-help
 communities

Track all promotional submissions and results in the campaign tracker



Social Media & Community

Create and schedule content for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and Pinterest using Canva brand templates — 3–5 posts per week per active brand

Manage Pinterest boards for brands — daily pinning with keyword-optimized descriptions

Engage authentically in relevant
 groups and 
 communities — value-first, never spam

Pitch BookTok and Bookstagram micro-influencers (500–20K followers) for organic promotion — track outreach in provided spreadsheet



 Marketing

Set up and maintain MailerLite automation sequences for all three author brands — using provided sequence briefs

Draft and schedule monthly newsletters for each brand — share with publisher for approval before sending

Monitor list growth, open rates, and click rates — include in weekly report



Weekly Reporting

Every Monday you send a one-page summary covering: sales and KU pages read by title, review counts and new reviews, ARC pipeline status, ad ACOS and spend, social media highlights, tasks completed, and priorities for the coming week. No surprises — if something is going wrong, you flag it immediately, not in the Monday report.



Required Qualifications

Minimum 1 year of hands-on Amazon KDP experience — you have personally managed titles through launch, not just performed uploads

Proven ARC campaign experience — you can describe a specific campaign: how many ARCs distributed, what outreach you ran, how many reviews resulted

Experience creating A+ Content — you can show examples

Amazon Ads experience — you know what ACOS means and how to improve it

Excellent written English — your outreach
s and community posts represent real author brands

Strong spreadsheet skills — you build clean, organized trackers and maintain them consistently

Reliable and proactive — you flag problems before they become crises, and you follow through without needing to be chased



Preferred Qualifications

Experience with BookBub submissions and Featured Deal applications

Familiarity with MailerLite or similar
 marketing platforms

Active knowledge of BookTok, Bookstagram, or homesteading/parenting online communities

Experience managing multiple titles or author brands simultaneously

Familiarity with Publisher Rocket or Book Bolt for keyword research



What We Provide

A detailed launch playbook for every title — you will always know exactly what to do and in what order

Budget for ARC tools, promotional campaigns, and Amazon Ads — you manage the spending, we approve the amounts

Clear weekly priorities and regular check-ins

A long-term role that grows with the catalog — for the right person, this is not a short contract



Confidentiality — Non-Negotiable

This role involves access to sensitive business information. All candidates sign a basic NDA before starting. Key requirements:



All pen name identities are strictly confidential — never disclosed to anyone under any circumstances

You will never confirm or suggest that any author name is a pseudonym, or that multiple brands are operated by the same person

Business strategy, revenue data, and manuscript content are proprietary — not to be shared or used outside this engagement

Upon termination for any reason, all access is revoked immediately and all credentials change on the same day



ONE MORE THING

Each author brand is a completely independent identity annd are separate authors in every interaction you have — with readers, reviewers, influencers, and communities. There is no 'parent company' that is ever visible or mentioned. If you are not comfortable maintaining this level of brand separation consistently, this role is not the right fit.



Compensation

Rate: $8–$12 USD per hour based on experience, or a negotiated monthly retainer for ongoing work. We prefer a retainer once the trial period is complete — it gives you income predictability and us budget certainty. A 13th month bonus is provided in December as standard practice.



How to Apply

Start your application with the phrase: Ready to launch.



Applications that do not begin with this phrase will not be reviewed. This confirms you read the full description before applying.



Then include all four of the following. Applications missing any element will not be reviewed:



KDP experience: Describe specifically what you have done on Amazon KDP. Name the types of books, the tasks you owned, how many titles, and for how long. One or two specific examples with real details.

ARC campaign example: Describe a specific ARC or outreach campaign you ran. What was the book or product, how many people did you reach, what was the response rate, and how did you track it?

Tracker sample: Share a link to or screenshot of a spreadsheet you have built to manage a similar workflow. It does not need to be KDP-specific — it needs to show you build organized, functional tracking systems.

Rate and availability: Your weekly availability in hours, your timezone, your hourly rate or monthly retainer expectation.



Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a 30-minute video interview and a paid trial task before any longer-term agreement is made.

title: Estimating Manager
description:
Job Summary 

The Estimating Manager is responsible for building a reliable, scalable estimating function that helps the company respond quickly to opportunities while maintaining accuracy, consistency, and strong bid quality. 



This role is not just responsible for producing takeoffs. It owns the estimating process from bid intake through preconstruction coordination, including resource planning, quote collection, bid tracking, quality review, and process improvement. The role should strengthen the company’s ability to handle more opportunities without sacrificing accuracy or overloading operating teams. 

 

The right person will combine electrical estimating judgment, strong process discipline, and practical use of software and AI tools to improve speed, visibility, and output quality. 





Key Responsibilities 

1. Estimating Operations 

- Lead the day-to-day estimating workflow, including bid intake, takeoff coordination, estimate development, and bid-status tracking. 

- Ensure bid packages and specifications are reviewed quickly and routed to the right internal or external estimating resources. 

-Maintain visibility on active bids, priorities, deadlines, and handoffs. 



2. Quality, Accuracy, and Preconstruction Support 

- Review estimates and takeoffs for completeness, accuracy, and alignment with scope before submission. 

- Coordinate RFIs, clarifications, subcontractor pricing, and material quotes needed to support sound bid submission. 

- Support post-award preconstruction documentation, including submittals and related handoff materials, where needed. 



3. System Building and Capacity Growth 

- Build and improve the estimating system, including playbooks, templates, workflows, and use of outside estimating support when appropriate. 

- Increase estimating throughput in a controlled way by improving process, resource coordination, and tool usage. 

- Identify practical opportunities to use software and AI tools to reduce manual work, improve consistency, and strengthen estimating output. 



Required Qualifications 

- 3 to 5+ years of construction estimating experience, with meaningful experience in electrical estimating or electrical takeoffs. 

- Strong ability to read construction drawings, specifications, and electrical plans and translate them into organized, accurate takeoffs and pricing support. 

- Demonstrated experience using at least one major digital takeoff or estimating platform effectively; ability to learn additional tools quickly. 

- Strong working proficiency in Excel and document management in a deadline-driven environment. 

- Experience coordinating quotes, bid information, and estimating deliverables across multiple projects at the same time. 

- Clear written and verbal English communication suitable for working with US-based leadership, vendors, and supply houses. 

- Self-directed and dependable in a remote work environment with time-sensitive deliverables. 

- Practical working knowledge of AI tools for business use, including using them to improve speed, organization, drafting, research support, or workflow quality. 



Preferred Qualifications 

- Experience working for a US-based electrical contractor, general contractor, or construction services firm. 

- Experience helping build or improve estimating processes, templates, or SOPs. 

- Familiarity with Bluebeam, PlanSwift, or similar takeoff and markup tools. 

- Experience working with outsourced estimating support or specialized consultants. 

- Degree or formal training in construction, engineering, or a related field. 



Core Values 

Success in this role requires alignment with the company’s core values: 



Trust Is Our Foundation 

- Communicate clearly and consistently on bid status, risks, and deadlines 

- Follow through on commitments and maintain reliability under time pressure 

 

Teamwork Is Our Edge 

- Coordinate effectively with operations, leadership, vendors, and external estimating support 

- Share information proactively to keep projects and bids moving forward 

 

Our Work Is Our Proof 

- Deliver accurate, well-organized, and professional estimating output 

- Take ownership of quality and continuously improve the standard of work 

 



Always Learning, Always Teaching 

- Continuously improve estimating methods, tools, and workflows 

- Share knowledge, templates, and improvements to strengthen the broader team

title: B2B Sales Development Assistant
description:
We are EventMinder — a real-time event operations platform that helps conference organizers manage live event workflows, including workshop waitlists, seat changes, and attendance tracking during events.



We work with conferences and event teams that run multi-session events and need better control over attendee flow, capacity, and real-time session management.



We are looking for a B2B Sales Development Assistant (SDR) to support structured outbound outreach and help book qualified discovery calls with event organizers.



What you will do

Execute structured B2B outreach using
 and LinkedIn (scripts and sequences provided)

Send outbound
s to conference organizers and event planners using a 3-step sequence

Use LinkedIn messaging for connection requests and follow-ups

Book qualified discovery calls directly into the calendar

Follow up consistently until a response is received

Conduct follow-up calls only on warm or engaged leads (no cold calling)

Maintain accurate tracking of outreach activity and pipeline updates

What we provide

Curated list of targeted conference organizers (no prospecting required)

Fully written
 sequences, LinkedIn messages, and call scripts

Clear outreach workflow and defined process

Structured system from day one (no guesswork)

What we are looking for

Proven experience in B2B cold outreach, appointment setting, or SDR roles

Strong written English communication skills

Comfortable executing structured outreach via
 and LinkedIn

Reliable, consistent, and detail-oriented

Ability to follow a defined system without deviation

Experience in SaaS, events, or lead generation is a plus

Expectations

Daily activity report (outreach sent, responses, and booked meetings)

Weekly check-in call (Zoom or phone)

Stable internet connection and professional work setup

Strict adherence to provided messaging and process

Focus on execution consistency over improvisation

Success in this role looks like

Consistent daily execution of structured
 + LinkedIn outreach

Accurate tracking of leads and responses

Steady flow of qualified discovery calls booked

Proper use of calls only for warm or engaged leads

Full compliance with the outreach system provided

Compensation



Competitive pay based on experience, to be discussed during the interview.



Engagement Type



Independent contractor position. Flexible hours, with an expectation of approximately 30 hours per week.



Application Screening Questions

1. Outreach Experience (Proof of execution)



Describe your direct experience with B2B outreach 
 and/or LinkedIn).

Include:



what you personally did (not your team)

daily/weekly volume 
s, messages, or calls)

any results (replies, meetings booked, demos set)

2. Process Discipline Check



We use a strict outreach system (scripts, sequences, and tracking). How do you ensure you follow a process exactly without deviating?



(We want to understand how you handle structure, not creativity.)
